message.content:
You are a DIRTY-ROOM integrator for the tamarin-rs relicensing campaign, working in /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs. Read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md (integration rules) and the relevant INTEGRATION_REPORT.md sections first.
RULES: adapters and extraction only — NEVER transplant ported logic into clean (headerless) files; clean vendored sources get mechanical fixes only. A clean file acquiring a GPL header from scripts/gen_license_headers.py is a provenance violation: stop and report. If a gap still blocks, KEEP the ported path + report precisely — never force a swap. Comments describe current code only. No git commits. Append a dated section to INTEGRATION_REPORT.md; return a plain-text summary.
FINAL VALIDATION: cargo build --workspace; cargo test -p tamarin-parser -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-prover -p tamarin-server; the wellformedness fixture suite; scripts/gen_license_headers.py then --check (expect 0 stale). Report the header-count delta and any author citations that disappeared.

Integrate the round-5 clean closures for units E (macros) and B (graph wrap trigger). Similarity audits passed for: A, B, E — only integrate passed units; keep-and-report the rest. Two dirty-room agents (G swap, D re-integration) ran before you — rebase on the CURRENT tree and re-run validation yourself.
- E (if passed): re-sync crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs from the round-5 workspace (it now has the consumer staged mode: acc-lemma/case-test formulas untouched, primary-rule-form only). Route macro_expand::expand_theory_macros / macro_expanded_clone through the clean staged entry. Gate: the three captured tests (bare-nullary; AccLemma and CaseTest non-expansion) pass UNMODIFIED, plus the whole theory suite and the wf pipeline fixtures (macro_expanded_clone feeds the wf checks — verify no regression). When green, DELETE the ported expand_theory_macros/expand_items/expand_rule in macro_expand.rs.
- B (if passed): re-sync crates/tamarin-server/src/graph_clean/ from the round-6 workspace (record-level wrap trigger closed). Wire the RawRule pre-rendered-cell seam: ported repr/simplify produce content, ported term printer renders cells, clean generate assembles + wraps + serializes. Rebuild the live byte gate the round-5 integrator used (INTEGRATION_REPORT.md round-5 section documents it: reference server, PATH /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin, ports 3200-3299, wide-record probe via interactive-graph-def/cases/raw — including the Ack( ~n.4, <x1.4, x2.4> ) wide-line case that previously failed) plus fresh captures across clustered/wide/induction/compressed variants; require Rust route output byte-equal to the reference. Update routes_graph::dot_output_for_a_simple_system to the reference dialect. When green, DELETE the ported serialization in handlers/dot.rs (and anything else the clean path now fully covers — abbreviation.rs only if the clean abbreviation engine serves the route end-to-end); keep repr.rs/simplify.rs (solver-side content) ported; keep-and-report residue precisely.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the foundational documents and understanding the current tree state.

content.content:
1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).
47

content.text: The working tree already has extensive changes from prior dirty-room agents. Let me read the relevant memory files and locate the round-5 report sections.

content.content: 1 --- 2 name: relicensing-campaign 3 description: "MIT-relicensing campaign state — per-file GPL headers applied, author/institution map, ranked rewrite clusters (2026-07-17)" 4 metadata: 5 node_type: memory 6 type: project 7 originSessionId: 57f8e1bc-d495-4b36-93c0-033347d5034a 8 --- 9 10 Campaign to relicense tamarin-rs from GPL-3 to MIT via permissions. State as of 2026-07-17: 11 12 - **Headers applied**: 131 Rust files in crates/ carry "Currently GPL 3.0 until granted permission by the following authors:" headers with per-file author lists (≥10 blamed lines in that file's cited upstream sources) + "Ported from upstream tamarin-prover sources:" provenance. Generated by scratchpad scripts (map_citations/resolve_blame/gen_headers.py); data in blame_map.json, header_report.json, tail_matrix.json, tail_classification.json (session scratchpad — copy out if needed). Kevin Milner excluded (self; was Oxford *student* so no s.11(2) issue). cargo check clean. 13 - **Uncited-file audit**: 35/39 uncited files genuinely independent; annotated_goals.rs + abstract_interpretation.rs were ported (headers added); routes.rs + json_resp.rs borderline wire-contract (left unheadered, flagged). 14 - **Sufficiency finding**: individual consent NOT sufficient for employed authors — DE UrhG §69b, CH URG Art.17 + ETH Act 36(2) (BGer 4A_317/2022), FR CPI L113-9(+ agents publics), UK CDPA s.11(2) vest economic rights in employers. ETH delegates OSS decisions to Research Group Leader (Basin can sign for ETH). Needed signatures: ETH (covers Meier/Schmidt-era, Basin, Sasse, Linker, Jérôme/Azurios, Maric), CISPA (Künnemann, Morio, Lukert, Luu, Ivanov, Medinger, Dax, Pallas, Wocker, prob. Dapprich, Hizbullah, Cremers-CISPA-era, Jacomme-CISPA), Inria/Loria (Dreier, Racouchot, Dumenil, Jacomme-Inria, Hirschi), + individuals. 15 - **Key identifications**: "Pops"/racoucho1u = Maïwenn Racouchot (Loria PhD → Inria-covered); "ValentinYuri" = Valentin Thiebaux (TELECOM Nancy, wrote macros feature incl. own (c) header); "Nynko" = Nicolas Beaudouin; gilcu3 = Reynaldo Gil Pons (U Luxembourg); symphorien = Guillaume Girol. Nancy course-students (Tom/BTom-GH, Aurand, sans-sucre, Thiebaux, Beaudouin, Colomb) likely NOT vested in Inria absent stagiaire agreements → individual asks via Dreier. True gaps: Artur Cygan (971 lines, Trail of Bits volunteer, reachable) and "Jackie"/Kanakanajm (74 lines, web-only, unknown). 16 - **De minimis (dismissible, verified mechanical)**: Felix Yan, Cohn-Gordon, Dumenil, Dax-residue, Wiggers, Takata, Pallas, Dayo, Taldykin, Wocker; partly: Hizbullah, Schoop, Maric. Substantive (not dismissible): symphorien, gilcu3, Nick Moore, Thiebaux, Beaudouin, Tom, Ivanov. 17 - **Rewrite clusters ranked** (destroy-and-reimplement black-box): #1 GRAPH/DOT (4.4k LOC, frees Dapprich+Aurand), #2 WELLFORMEDNESS wf.rs (3.2k, frees sans-sucre/Nynko/Tom-slices, easiest — independent lint checks), #3 WEB LAYER (5.3k, frees Jackie+Colomb fully, Cygan's 419-line slice). TRAPS: SubtermStore rewrite is a red herring (Lukert's algorithm lives in simplify.rs/reduction.rs, deep solver logic, not specifiable — but Lukert is CISPA-covered anyway); Tactics rewrite unnecessary (Racouchot Inria-covered). 18 - **Clean-room (2026-07-17)**: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/ with PROTOCOL.md (audit-trail doc), per-cluster SPEC.md + oracles. wf: wf_oracle.sh wraps HS binary (tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/.../bin/tamarin-prover, needs linuxbrew PATH for maude), 37 pre-captured outputs, stripped ast_types.rs interop header, required_api.md (boundary: WfError{topic,message}, check_theory, topics, underline_topic, render_report + 6 secondary fns). graph/web: hs_server.sh live oracle + symlink to scripts/.web_hs_cache (1.8G, 81 manifests {base,lemmas,log,capped,manifest}). Round-1 agents launched (workflow wf_d28aad0e-98c); transcripts retained as non-access evidence. Pipeline after: transcript audit (grep for forbidden reads) → similarity audit vs HS source → byte-parity acceptance → dirty-room writes only thin adapters, never transplants logic. Caveats told to user: model training data may include GPL source (mitigated by no-recall instruction + similarity audit); audits must send back behavioral redo-instructions, not patches. 19 - **Clean-room round 1 COMPLETE (2026-07-17)**, all transcripts audited zero-violation (4 agents, no forbidden reads, no web fetches): wf-clean 1269 LOC 29/29 tests, 13/14 topics byte-parity (gap: Maude-coupled derivation checks — belongs to deriv_check cluster anyway); graph-clean 1376 LOC 18/18, byte-exact DOT + abbreviation engine (gaps: abbreviation SELECTION threshold not black-box determinable — needs parity-failure iteration; colors carried as data; system→graph content is solver-side, correctly out of rendering scope); web-clean 1005 LOC + 334 parity tests, 37/37, byte-parity over 2450 captured JSON envelope bodies + theory-view/proof-script/forms/404 HTML. Remaining pipeline: similarity audit (both-sides reviewer vs HS source, redo-instructions only) → byte-parity corpus acceptance → integration adapters, delete old modules + their headers (removes Jackie, Colomb, sans-sucre, most Dapprich/Aurand, Cygan's 419-line web slice from permission list). StructuredOutput retry-cap failures recur on long Opus agent reports — use file-based REPORT.md + plain text instead of schema for long-output agents. 20 - **Similarity audit PASSED all three crates, zero findings** (2026-07-17): wf clean code diverges from old port at every unconstrained point (independent-derivation evidence); graph source-only machinery absent; process note — future interop headers must convey behavior, not port seams (required_api.md leaked insert_wf_before/after_public_names_topics). 21 - **wf INTEGRATED in working tree**: crates/tamarin-parser/src/wf.rs → wf/ module (clean sources, mechanical path fixes only). Builds clean, 67/67 unit tests; fixture test fails on 6 topics the clean round-1 missed (Left/Right rule + Reserved prefixes = diff-mode my-fault corpus filter; Lemma annotations, Fresh public constants, Multiplication restriction; + guardedness false-positive on exists_trace_reuse) — wf round-2 agent running with staged round2/ inputs; wf_oracle.sh now passes extra args (--diff). Old wf.rs recoverable from git HEAD. 22 - **graph-clean serializer validated 12022/12022 byte-exact** over ALL unique DOT payloads extracted from the 81 manifests (oracle/dot_corpus/). Remaining gap: abbreviation SELECTION policy — round-2 agent mining paired json(full-term)/dot(abbreviated) captures for the decision rule. Web round-2 agent extending page generality (3 → all ~15k captured HTML bodies, per-family coverage map). 23 - **wf cluster RETIRED (2026-07-17)**: round-2 closed all 6 missing topics + guardedness false-positive (transcript audited clean). Integrated: parser tests 69/69, oracle harness 21/21 Tamarin-match, workspace builds, server suite 96 green. Old wf.rs deleted (git HEAD has it); new wf/ carries NO GPL header. Integration glue: wf/order.rs adapter (WF_TOPIC_ORDER + WF_AFTER_* splice anchors, behavior-content) + `pub mod order; pub use order::*;` appended to wf/mod.rs — RE-ADD BOTH when re-syncing clean sources (sync recipe: sed path fixes crate::pretty→super:: etc., see transcript). Incremental similarity audit of round-2 code running. BACKLOG: "Fact capitalization issues" topic exists in old anchor list — probe oracle behaviorally in a future wf round (fixtures don't cover it; clean crate may lack the check). sans-sucre/Nynko/Tom/Aurand/Dax wf lines no longer in any shipped ported file. 24 - **Graph/web round-2 gates ALL PASSED (2026-07-17)**: graph cracked abbreviation selection (rendered len ≥10 ∧ occ ≥2 in whole system ∧ non-tuple, bottom-up; corpus-exact over 97,538 legends + live boundary probes; residual: AC/DH terms counted over solver-normalised forms, proof-state dependent, ~7% of exceptions characterized). Web: 15,178/15,178 captured HTML bodies byte-exact (only two template families exist: intdot + overview shell; west proofscript pane modeled opaque except Chaum). Transcript audits clean; similarity audits pass 0 findings — HS judgeTerm weight/greedy/soft-limit machinery absent from clean side (different choice at every latitude point). Integration agent running (vendor graph_clean/web_clean into tamarin-server, adapters at system_to_dot/render_svg_or_dot_with + theory_html callers, delete abbreviation.rs/dot.rs-serialization/theory_html.rs; repr.rs+simplify.rs+options.rs stay ported/headered pending graph round-3 on simplification transforms). Gate: server 96-test suite incl. captured-HS parity fixtures. 25 - **Integration outcome (2026-07-17)**: PARTIAL, protocol-faithful. graph_clean/web_clean vendored into tamarin-server (1,842 headerless LOC, byte-verified vs cleanroom); intdot/add+delete-forms/local-overview-shell rewired (~58 GPL LOC deleted); dot.rs + abbreviation.rs + theory_html.rs KEPT — graph swap blocked on 3 clean-side gaps (record-cell term rendering, trapezium shapes, graph-global id/port allocator; all corpus-observable → graph round-3), web swap on 2 (non-local shell variant, dynamic textHeight). All suites green (verified independently: build 0 errors, server 80+53+…, parser, captured-HS parity). INTEGRATION_REPORT.md in cleanroom/. 26 - **Cygan discount (2026-07-17)**: his ~971 in-range lines are ALL mechanical modernization (lens→record-dot etc., verified per-commit); net-new ≈5–8 thin lines; underlying expression belongs to Meier/Künnemann/Morio/Ivanov/core (covered). Cygan OFF the ask-list. Post-A–G individual ask-list = symphorien (46) + Nick Moore (10) ONLY. 27 - **Scope doc**: cleanroom/TAIL_ELIMINATION_SCOPE.md — units A–H ranked (A web-handlers frees Jackie/Colomb/near-zeros Cremers; B graph repr+simplify+3 blockers; C wf-completion; D console; E macros; F injective; G deriv-checks; H theory-object/Luu = skip). NOT launched per user instruction (scope only). Working tree has ~134 modified files uncommitted (headers + wf swap + integration) — suggest checkpoint commit. 28 - **Evidence published (2026-07-17)**: github.com/kmilner/tamarin-cleanroom (public), commit d656c403434df80a51f0cc3bad90f5fa3465a1b3 — protocol, specs, clean workspaces, implementer+auditor transcripts, audits, dot_corpus.tar.gz, full SHA256SUMS (300 files). WITHHELD from public mirror (gitignored, local-only, hash-anchored in the published SHA256SUMS): main-session transcript, research workflows wf_53f48d60/wf_8b8fd87b/wf_5ec90b24, Cygan analysis, affiliation outputs, TAIL_ELIMINATION_SCOPE.md — privacy (de-pseudonymizes contributors) + strategy. Local evidence master: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/evidence/. Main-repo ~134-file checkpoint commit still pending. 29 - **Units A–G LAUNCHED (2026-07-17, user-approved)**: 7 parallel clean-room agents, workflow wf_7f807521-670. New cluster dirs: console/ (D, CLI surface), macros/ (E, 8 macro examples), injective/ (F, --precompute-only oracle confirmed), derivcheck/ (G, callback-trait architecture — solver stays ported); rounds-3 in weblayer/ (A: handler state machine over ProverOps trait + non-local shell + textarea rows; ports 3100-3199) and graphdot/ (B: term-cell rendering, shapes, id/port allocator, content mapping, simplification levels; ports 3200-3299); wellformedness/ (C: reducible-formula-terms w/ caller-supplied reducible set, guardedness wrap, Fact-capitalization probe). Pipeline per unit after: transcript audit → similarity audit → integrate (serialize per crate) → validate → delete+deheader. User committed the main-repo checkpoint themselves. 30 - **Header tooling (2026-07-17)**: scripts/gen_license_headers.py + committed scripts/header_identities.json (curated: rkunnema/xaDxelA merges). GitHub usernames; module-path citations only in "port of" context; idempotent; `--check` = CI gate + tripwire (clean file acquiring a header = provenance violation). Headers ARE the provenance tracker. 31 - **USER DIRECTION — drop `-clean` suffixes**: no tracking value (headers track provenance). Rename at each unit's integration when the natural name frees up (wf/ is the model — integrated suffix-free). graph_clean→graph after Unit B deletes ported graph/; web_clean→web at Unit A. Cleanroom workspace dirs keep names until campaign ends (running agents + published evidence reference them). 32 - **A–G implementation rounds COMPLETE (2026-07-17), transcripts all clean (586 calls, 0 violations)**: A dispatch.rs Server — version-map semantics live-derived, textarea rows=(newlines+2) solved, non-local shell unobservable on one host (parameterized hook, honest negative), 55 tests. B generation complete — NodeIdAllocator 12022/12022, record-cell renderer, invtrapezium discovered live, system→graph mapping byte-exact end-to-end, simplification via cookie/URL params (L1/2/3 byte-identical = gap; wrap DECISION + compression content = solver gaps), 44 tests. C 53 tests, 12 fixtures — formula_terms rewrite w/ reducible set, wrapped formula printer, Fact-capitalization topic CONFIRMED + implemented, residual: wide-rule wrapping. D cli-clean byte-exact CLI surface 22 tests + 12/12 binary matches. E macro expansion semantics (capture-avoiding, sort-exact matching, DAG transitive) 14 tests + 6 parity fixtures. F injfacts-clean 32 tests, decision rule from probe batches; corpus tally lost with agent /tmp — validation continuation running. G derivcheck-clean DerivabilitySolver trait, 297-byte capture cmp-identical, 15 tests. NOTE: agents reported REPORT-file writes "blocked by harness" — content folded into BEHAVIOR.md instead (fine). Gates workflow wf_bfd5a6a0-31b running: 3 batched similarity auditors + F corpus validation. Then: integrations serialized per crate (suffix-free names per user direction). 33 - **Gates round COMPLETE (2026-07-17)**: ALL similarity audits pass, 0 findings across A/B/C/D/E/F/G deltas (close call cleared: textarea-rows formula output-forced, provenance proves observation). F corpus validation: 36/36 trace agree, 0 mismatch (diff theories n/a — injective analysis is trace-only, an oracle finding). F-validation transcript audited clean. Integration wave running (wf_62e2c0ef-a3e): server branch = B-simplification-reprobe (cookie methodology hint per user lead) → delta audit → A+B integration (rename graph_clean→graph, web_clean→web, ProverOps adapter, delete ported handlers/graph); theory branch = C/D/E/F/G sequential (wf re-sync preserving order.rs, cli/ vendor, macro/injective/derivcheck adapters — DerivabilitySolver implemented over ported solver). Both end with gen_license_headers.py run. 34 - **Unit B simplification-level gap — CLOSED as verified negative (2026-07-17 re-probe)**: the level NUMBER is inert. Served JS turns a client-side cookie into the graph-URL param; server emits NO Set-Cookie anywhere; URL param is the only channel and IS honored. The off/L1/L2/L3 menu is binary: "off" = UI adds uncompact=&uncompress=; L1/L2/L3 identical. Real transforms are the flags (uncompress 39→74 nodes, uncompact enriches records, unabbreviate drops legend). User's cookie lead was right about the mechanism (cookie exists client-side) but the level itself does nothing. Delta audit pass 0 findings; audit noted clean "levels inert" conclusion CONTRADICTS the HS source (simplifySystem really branches i==2/i==3 transitive reduction) = affirmative non-access evidence. Also cracked: record-cell wrap = 87-column paragraph fill, functor-invariant (residual: fsep one-element lookahead + delimiter-peel characterized, not byte-implemented). 35 - **Integration wave OUTCOME (2026-07-17): all green, but NET-ZERO DELETIONS — header count 134, unchanged.** Both integrators kept-and-reported per protocol. Verified independently: build 0 errors; parser/theory/prover/server suites all green (483+96+68+67+…); GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS 12,022/12,022; headers --check 0 stale. Per-unit blockers (detail in INTEGRATION_REPORT.md + TAIL_ELIMINATION_SCOPE.md round-3/4 correction): A = Route::parse subset + ProverOps needs ~13 fragment-producers from 4k-LOC axum handlers; B = generate() lacks role-clusters/wrap-wiring/LastAction/trapezium + LNTerm bridge + emits web-payload DOT dialect vs ported image dialect (`digraph G`/``, no in-repo byte oracle); C = check_terms KEPT (clean drops node-binder `Free x` distinction — oracle-observable, closable) though wf/ resync landed; D = --help/--version now byte-identical through clean cli/ but parse_args/run.rs kept (Rust-only flags); E = rewire REVERTED (nullary-macro under-expansion, AccLemma/CaseTest over-expansion — closable); F = ARCHITECTURAL (solver consumes ported MonotonicBehaviour internals the tag-set contract can't supply — needs design decision, not probing); G = five mismatches (mostly closable). Ask-list arithmetic UNCHANGED until real deletions. Cheapest path: gap-closure rounds C/E/G → D residue; A/B need integration-shaped rounds; F blocked. Headers now use GitHub usernames repo-wide (identities refreshed, 64 cached; gen_license_headers false-positive fixed: cli/errors.rs Batch.hs is compat OUTPUT, split across concat!). 36 - **Evidence updated + pushed (2026-07-17)**: cleanroom commit 63ed8a9 — round-3/4 transcripts (7 implementers, 4 auditors, 2 integrators, F-validation, B re-probe), cleanroom_audit_results.txt = 16 transcripts/1339 calls/0 violations, SHA256SUMS refreshed 300→727 (all originals retained; NOTE: SUMS covers the WHOLE repo minus .git/target/dot_corpus, not just evidence/). wf_7f807521-670 has NO persisted launch script (prompts live in transcript first messages; noted in MANIFEST). 37 - **Closure wave 1 (wf_1741f4ad-48d, 2026-07-17): FIRST REAL DELETION — check_terms.rs gone, headers 134→133.** All 6 clean rounds (C4/D4/E4/G4/B5/A4) implemented + audited pass 0 findings + transcripts clean (22 total/1893 calls/0 violations, evidence @8901219). C FULLY swapped: formula_reports routed through clean wf (new wf_adapt.rs adapters incl. normalize_temporal_sorts — parser leaves bare `@ i` Untagged, spuriously flags without it); check_terms.rs + check_guarded_wf DELETED, byte-verified vs oracle. C4 also discovered+implemented the unlisted `Quantifier sorts` topic. Remaining blockers refined: D = coupled re-sync (round-4 changed vendored-module APIs together; needs adapt.rs redo + ~50-field Args mapping + run.rs stream rewrite — pure dirty work); E = staging mismatch (HS macro stage DOES expand acc-lemma/case-test formulas but RS staged pass must not — clean needs staged mode; also no variants recursion); G = adapter needs nullary Var→App normalization + MDC corpus gate (dirty work); B = wrap TRIGGER is record-level accumulated-column (Wadler group/fits), NOT per-cell-87 — live-confirmed flat-25 cell wraps deep on wide line; B5 closed clusters (free-then-clustered id bucketing), #last/Temporal nodes, RawRule pre-rendered-cell seam; trapezium UNOBSERVED (Shaped escape hatch added); A = del/path+verify exist but take two-segment sub-paths (round-4 probe-shape artifact; fixtures are genuine HS captures — staged to weblayer/round5/ with ORACLE_NOTES.md). Server integrator also completed edit-form closure byte-identical to live HS (incl. HS's stray ). 38 - **Closure wave 2 LAUNCHED (wf_60530b15-4b6)**: clean A5 (del/path+verify via staged captures)/B6 (record-level wrap trigger + opportunistic color derivation)/E5 (staged mode) + audits ‖ dirty G-swap (adapter normalization + corpus gate → delete deriv_check.rs) then D-swap (coupled re-integration → delete ported parse/framing); then integ1 (E+B swaps incl. live graph byte gate) → integ2 (A full Server adoption, ProverOps extraction, delete routes.rs/state.rs, rename web_clean→web). Audit baseline for wave-2 deltas: cleanroom HEAD 8901219. 39 - Related: [[intruder-variants-licensing]], [[tamarin-rs-repo]]. 40

1022	## G.2 The two dirty-room items (both done in the adapter)
1023	
1024	1. **Nullary-Var->App input normalization.** The parser leaves a bare identifier
1025	   naming a declared nullary function as `Term::Var(name)`; the clean candidate
1026	   collector treats a constant as `App(name,[])`. The adapter rewrites
1027	   `Var(name)->App(name,[])` for every `name` in the theory's declared-nullary set
1028	   (user `functions: f/0` + builtin nullary constants, via the extracted
1029	   `collect_all_nullary_fun_names`), on rule facts + let-blocks + macro bodies,
1030	   before feeding the clean check. Behaviour-neutral; matches the ported
1031	   name-based deny-list. Verified: `mdc_nullary` (a `c/0` constant is not flagged).
1032	
1033	   The same normalization pass ALSO folds each variable's sort onto its class
1034	   representative (`Suffix(X)->X`, `Untagged->Msg`) — the exact classes the ported
1035	   `sort_ord` deduped/ordered by. This is required because the clean crate keys
1036	   variable identity on the full `VarSpec` and its `render_variable`/`var_order_key`
1037	   do not special-case `Suffix(_)`. Without it a `x:nat` renders `x` not `%x`, and
1038	   a variable appearing as both bare `c` and `c:msg` lists twice. Verified by the
1039	   discriminating `mdc_sorts_suffix` fixture (`In(h(<a:fresh,b:nat,c:msg>))` with a
1040	   bare-`a,b,c` action): oracle `~a, a, b, c, %b` reproduced byte-for-byte
1041	   (one `c`, index-primary then Fresh<Msg<Nat ordering). Macro bodies get the
1042	   nullary rewrite but NOT the sort fold, so macro-argument substitution still
1043	   matches by identity.
1044	
1045	2. **Batched `DerivabilitySolver` over the ported solver.** `check_rule(&RuleProbe)`
1046	   resolves public/timepoint candidates as trivially derivable without probing
1047	   (they never belong in the synthesised `Fr(..)` premises, exactly as the ported
1048	   `collect_rule_free_vars` pub/node exclusion), then synthesises ONE probe theory
1049	   for the remaining candidates and calls `prove_probe` ONCE — a single saturation
1050	   answering all of the rule's variables. Non-`Solved` (incl. deadline) maps to
1051	   `NotDerivable`, reproducing the ported conservative timeout policy exactly (the
1052	   clean `TimeoutPolicy` branch is therefore never taken); an elaboration failure
1053	   (`None`) leaves the whole rule unreported, as the ported `continue` did.

1056	## G.3 Corpus gate — and the latent ported bug it caught
1057	
1058	Gate = the MDC topic block of RS vs the v1.13.0 oracle (`hs_oracle.sh` binary,
1059	`/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin` on PATH), byte-compared with `cat -A`.
1060

1062	diverged from the oracle on EVERY MDC theory by exactly one byte-line: the ported
1063	`format_deriv_report` emitted a single `\n` between the `====` underline and the
1064	intro paragraph, where the oracle emits a blank line (`====\n\n  The variables`).
1065	The clean `render_block` produces the blank line, so routing through it FIXES the
1066	divergence. The bug had never been caught because the wf fixture suite
1067	intentionally omits the MDC topic (tests/wellformedness_fixtures/expected.txt:8)
1068	and no captured test exercised it.
1069

1071	`mdc_private` (private destructor), `mdc_multivar` (two rules, multi-var ordering),
1072	`mdc_macro` (theory `macros:`), `mdc_let` (rule `let{}`), `mdc_sorts_prefix`
1073	(`~x`,`%n`), `mdc_sorts_suffix` (`a:fresh`,`b:nat`,`c:msg` + bare), `mdc_pub` (a
1074	`$p` resolved derivable while a msg var is flagged), `mdc_nodcheck`
1075	(`[no_derivcheck]` skip), `mdc_nullary` (`c/0` not flagged), plus the canonical
1076	real example `features/derivation-checks/revealingSignatureDerivationTest`.
