1	//! Unit E — `.spthy` macro expansion (clean-room).
2	//!
3	//! Two entry points, differing only in how much of the theory a single call is
4	//! allowed to touch:
5	//!  * [`expand`] — full-close view: every macro use at every use site (including
6	//!    acc-lemma / case-test formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant and
7	//!    left/right diff rule forms) is replaced by its transitively-substituted
8	//!    body.
9	//!  * [`expand_staged`] — the consumer's parse-stage view. Same expansion of
10	//!    ordinary lemmas / restrictions / predicates / processes / the PRIMARY rule
11	//!    form (and bare-nullary uses), but two carve-outs dictated by the consumer's
12	//!    pipeline staging: acc-lemma and case-test formulas are left UNTOUCHED (a
13	//!    later consumer stage owns their expansion [Q41]), and only the primary rule
14	//!    form is rewritten — derived variant / left-right rule forms are not
15	//!    recursed into, matching the parse-stage fact that a rule then exists only
16	//!    in its primary form [Q42].
17	//!
18	//! In both, the `macros:` declarations are retained in place (with their
19	//! original, unexpanded bodies). See ../BEHAVIOR.md for the observed semantics
20	//! this implements; every rule below traces to a `[Qn]` observation.
21	//!
22	//! Summary of the semantics implemented here:
23	//!  * a macro `name(f1..fk) = body`; call `name(a1..ak)` binds `fi := ai` and
24	//!    substitutes **simultaneously** (parallel / capture-avoiding)          [Q7]
25	//!  * a bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is a parenthesis-free use
26	//!    of that macro and resolves to its body; `~x`/`$x` and names of arity>=1
27	//!    macros are ordinary variables and do not resolve                      [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]
28	//!  * a formal matches a body variable by **full identity incl. sort**; `~x`
29	//!    and `$x` do NOT match an untagged formal `x` and stay free            [Q27,Q28]
30	//!  * a nullary macro reserves its name against a same-named formal: inside a
31	//!    body the name resolves to the macro, not the formal                   [Q36]
32	//!  * bodies may call only strictly-earlier macros ⇒ the macro dependency
33	//!    graph is a DAG, so expansion always terminates                        [Q8,Q19,Q20]
34	//!  * expansion is transitive: a body's own macro calls are expanded too    [Q9,Q18]
35	//!  * at the AST level a call's arg-count already equals the macro arity
36	//!    (the parser packs/rejects mismatches); `expand` no-ops on a mismatch)  [Q11,Q12,Q15,Q16,Q17]
37	//!  * the `macros:` declaration block is preserved unchanged in the output;
38	//!    the reference retains it in its pretty output and the consuming
39	//!    pipeline requires it in place                                          [Q37]
40	
41	pub mod ast;
42	
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use ast::*;
45	
46	/// Table of macros whose bodies are already fully expanded (macro-free),
47	/// keyed by macro name. Formals are matched against body variables by full
48	/// `VarSpec` identity, so the substitution map is keyed by `VarSpec`.
49	#[derive(Default)]
50	struct MacroTable {
51	    by_name: HashMap<String, (Vec<VarSpec>, Term)>,
52	}
53	
54	impl MacroTable {
55	    fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&(Vec<VarSpec>, Term)> {
56	        self.by_name.get(name)
57	    }
58	}
59	
60	/// How much of the theory one expansion pass is allowed to touch. The two modes
61	/// share the whole term/formula/rule traversal and differ only at the two
62	/// consumer-staging carve-outs (acc-lemma & case-test formulas; derived rule
63	/// forms).
64	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
65	enum Mode {
66	    /// Full-close view: expand every use site, including acc-lemma / case-test
67	    /// formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant / left-right diff rule forms.
68	    FullClose,
69	    /// Consumer's parse-stage view: leave acc-lemma & case-test formulas
70	    /// untouched (a later stage owns them [Q41]) and rewrite only the primary
71	    /// rule form, not derived variant / left-right forms [Q42].
72	    Staged,
73	}
74	
75	/// Full-close macro expansion: expand every macro use at every use site, keeping
76	/// the `macros:` declarations in place.
77	///
78	/// The result, fed back to the oracle, reproduces the reasoning content the
79	/// oracle computes for the original (modulo-AC variants / expanded-formula /
80	/// guarded-formula), which is byte-identical to a hand-inlined equivalent
81	/// (verified by workspace/byteparity.sh and workspace/formula_parity.sh). The
82	/// `macros:` declaration items pass through unchanged (with their original,
83	/// unexpanded bodies): the reference retains the block in its pretty output and
84	/// the consuming pipeline requires it in place [Q37].
85	pub fn expand(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
86	    expand_with(theory, Mode::FullClose)
87	}
88	
89	/// Staged (parse-stage) macro expansion — the entry the consumer's pipeline
90	/// calls.
91	///
92	/// Identical to [`expand`] except for two carve-outs the consumer's staging
93	/// requires, because it invokes this pass before a later stage that owns them:
94	///  * acc-lemma (`... accounts for "..."`) and case-test (`test <name>: "..."`)
95	///    formulas are left byte-identical — untouched — so the later stage can
96	///    expand them itself [Q41];
97	///  * rules exist only in their primary form at this stage, so only the primary
98	///    premises / actions / conclusions / let-block / embedded restrictions are
99	///    rewritten; derived variant and left-right diff rule forms are not recursed
100	///    into [Q42].
101	///
102	/// Everything else — ordinary lemmas, restrictions, predicates, processes,
103	/// equations, the primary rule form, and bare-nullary resolution — is expanded
104	/// exactly as in full close. The `macros:` declarations pass through unchanged
105	/// [Q37].
106	pub fn expand_staged(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
107	    expand_with(theory, Mode::Staged)
108	}
109	
110	fn expand_with(theory: &Theory, mode: Mode) -> Theory {
111	    let table = build_table(theory);
112	    let items = theory.items.iter().map(|it| expand_item(it, &table, mode)).collect();
113	    Theory {
114	        is_diff: theory.is_diff,
115	        name: theory.name.clone(),
116	        configuration: theory.configuration.clone(),
117	        items,
118	    }
119	}
120	
121	/// Build the macro table with fully-expanded (macro-free) bodies.
122	///
123	/// Macros are processed in declaration order; each body is expanded against the
124	/// macros defined strictly before it. Forward/self/mutual references are parse
125	/// errors upstream [Q8,Q19,Q20], so "earlier only" is complete and cannot loop.
126	fn build_table(theory: &Theory) -> MacroTable {
127	    let mut table = MacroTable::default();
128	    for item in &theory.items {
129	        if let TheoryItem::Macros(ms) = item {
130	            for m in ms {
131	                let body = expand_term(&m.body, &table);
132	                table.by_name.insert(m.name.clone(), (m.args.clone(), body));
133	            }
134	        }
135	    }
136	    table
137	}
138	
139	// ---- term-level expansion -------------------------------------------------
140	
141	/// Bottom-up macro expansion of a term. Arguments are expanded first, then a
142	/// macro call is replaced by its body with formals bound to the expanded args.
143	fn expand_term(t: &Term, table: &MacroTable) -> Term {
144	    match t {
145	        // A bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is that macro used
146	        // without parentheses; resolve it to the (already-expanded) body. A
147	        // fresh/pub-sorted name or the name of an arity>=1 macro is an ordinary
148	        // variable and is left untouched [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]. A nullary macro
149	        // reserves its name even against a same-named formal [Q36].
150	        Term::Var(v) => match table.get(&v.name) {
151	            Some((formals, body)) if v.sort == SortHint::Untagged && formals.is_empty() => {
152	                body.clone()
153	            }
154	            _ => t.clone(),
155	        },
156	
157	        Term::PubLit(_)
158	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
159	        | Term::NatLit(_)
160	        | Term::Number(_)
161	        | Term::NumberOne
162	        | Term::NatOne
163	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
164	
165	        Term::App(name, args) => {
166	            let args: Vec<Term> = args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect();
167	            if let Some((formals, body)) = table.get(name) {
168	                // At the AST level arg-count == arity for a valid parse. If it
169	                // ever differs (defensive), leave the call unexpanded.
170	                if formals.len() == args.len() {
171	                    let subst = build_subst(formals, &args);
172	                    // `body` is already macro-free; a simultaneous substitution
173	                    // of macro-free args therefore yields a macro-free term.
174	                    return substitute_term(body, &subst);
175	                }
176	            }
177	            Term::App(name.clone(), args)
178	        }
179	
180	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
181	            name.clone(),
182	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
183	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
184	        ),
185	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| expand_term(x, table)).collect()),
186	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
187	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
188	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
189	        ),
190	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
191	            *op,
192	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
193	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
194	        ),
195	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(expand_term(a, table))),
196	    }
197	}
198	
199	/// Build the simultaneous substitution `formal_i := arg_i`, keyed by the full
200	/// `VarSpec` of each formal (so sort/idx participate in matching) [Q27,Q28].
201	fn build_subst(formals: &[VarSpec], args: &[Term]) -> HashMap<VarSpec, Term> {
202	    formals
203	        .iter()
204	        .cloned()
205	        .zip(args.iter().cloned())
206	        .collect()
207	}
208	
209	/// Apply a simultaneous substitution to `t`. A `Var` that matches a key is
210	/// replaced by the bound term **without** re-descending into it (parallel
211	/// substitution — no capture) [Q7]. Non-matching variables (incl. differently
212	/// sorted `~x`/`$x`) are left untouched [Q27,Q28].
213	fn substitute_term(t: &Term, subst: &HashMap<VarSpec, Term>) -> Term {
214	    match t {
215	        Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(v) {
216	            Some(bound) => bound.clone(),
217	            None => t.clone(),
218	        },
219	        Term::PubLit(_)
220	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
221	        | Term::NatLit(_)
222	        | Term::Number(_)
223	        | Term::NumberOne
224	        | Term::NatOne
225	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
226	        Term::App(name, args) => Term::App(
227	            name.clone(),
228	            args.iter().map(|a| substitute_term(a, subst)).collect(),
229	        ),
230	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
231	            name.clone(),
232	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
233	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
234	        ),
235	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| substitute_term(x, subst)).collect()),
236	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
237	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
238	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
239	        ),
240	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
241	            *op,
242	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
243	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
244	        ),
245	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst))),
246	    }
247	}
248	
249	// ---- structural recursion through the theory ------------------------------
250	
251	fn expand_fact(f: &Fact, table: &MacroTable) -> Fact {
252	    Fact {
253	        persistent: f.persistent,
254	        name: f.name.clone(),
255	        args: f.args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
256	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
257	    }
258	}
259	
260	fn expand_atom(a: &Atom, table: &MacroTable) -> Atom {
261	    match a {
262	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => Atom::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
263	        Atom::Less(x, y) => Atom::Less(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
264	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => Atom::LessMset(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
265	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => Atom::Subterm(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
266	        Atom::Action(f, t) => Atom::Action(expand_fact(f, table), expand_term(t, table)),
267	        Atom::Last(t) => Atom::Last(expand_term(t, table)),
268	        Atom::Pred(f) => Atom::Pred(expand_fact(f, table)),
269	    }
270	}
271	
272	fn expand_formula(phi: &Formula, table: &MacroTable) -> Formula {
273	    match phi {
274	        Formula::False => Formula::False,
275	        Formula::True => Formula::True,
276	        Formula::Atom(a) => Formula::Atom(expand_atom(a, table)),
277	        Formula::Not(p) => Formula::Not(Box::new(expand_formula(p, table))),
278	        Formula::And(p, q) => Formula::And(
279	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
280	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
281	        ),
282	        Formula::Or(p, q) => Formula::Or(
283	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
284	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
285	        ),
286	        Formula::Implies(p, q) => Formula::Implies(
287	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
288	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
289	        ),
290	        Formula::Iff(p, q) => Formula::Iff(
291	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
292	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
293	        ),
294	        Formula::Forall(vs, p) => {
295	            Formula::Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
296	        }
297	        Formula::Exists(vs, p) => {
298	            Formula::Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
299	        }
300	    }
301	}
302	
303	fn expand_let(b: &LetBinding, table: &MacroTable) -> LetBinding {
304	    // A macro call may appear in the let value [Q23]; the bound pattern `var`
305	    // is a variable/pattern and is expanded too for uniformity.
306	    LetBinding {
307	        var: expand_term(&b.var, table),
308	        value: expand_term(&b.value, table),
309	    }
310	}
311	
312	fn expand_rule(r: &Rule, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> Rule {
313	    Rule {
314	        name: r.name.clone(),
315	        modulo: r.modulo.clone(),
316	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
317	        let_block: r.let_block.iter().map(|b| expand_let(b, table)).collect(),
318	        premises: r.premises.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
319	        actions: r.actions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
320	        conclusions: r.conclusions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
321	        embedded_restrictions: r
322	            .embedded_restrictions
323	            .iter()
324	            .map(|p| expand_formula(p, table))
325	            .collect(),
326	        // Derived rule forms. Full close expands them — the `(modulo AC)` variant
327	        // and left/right diff projections show the expansion [Q3,Q4,Q26]. In the
328	        // staged view a rule exists only in its primary form [Q42], so these are
329	        // carried through untouched.
330	        variants: match mode {
331	            Mode::FullClose => r.variants.iter().map(|v| expand_rule(v, table, mode)).collect(),
332	            Mode::Staged => r.variants.clone(),
333	        },
334	        left_right: match mode {
335	            Mode::FullClose => r.left_right.as_ref().map(|(l, rr)| {
336	                (
337	                    Box::new(expand_rule(l, table, mode)),
338	                    Box::new(expand_rule(rr, table, mode)),
339	                )
340	            }),
341	            Mode::Staged => r.left_right.clone(),
342	        },
343	    }
344	}
345	
346	fn expand_sapic_action(a: &SapicAction, table: &MacroTable) -> SapicAction {
347	    match a {
348	        SapicAction::New(v) => SapicAction::New(v.clone()),
349	        SapicAction::Insert(k, v) => {
350	            SapicAction::Insert(expand_term(k, table), expand_term(v, table))
351	        }
352	        SapicAction::Delete(k) => SapicAction::Delete(expand_term(k, table)),
353	        SapicAction::ChIn { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChIn {
354	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
355	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
356	        },
357	        SapicAction::ChOut { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChOut {
358	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
359	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
360	        },
361	        SapicAction::Lock(t) => SapicAction::Lock(expand_term(t, table)),
362	        SapicAction::Unlock(t) => SapicAction::Unlock(expand_term(t, table)),
363	        SapicAction::Event(f) => SapicAction::Event(expand_fact(f, table)),
364	        SapicAction::Msr { prems, acts, concs, restrictions } => SapicAction::Msr {
365	            prems: prems.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
366	            acts: acts.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
367	            concs: concs.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
368	            restrictions: restrictions.iter().map(|p| expand_formula(p, table)).collect(),
369	        },
370	    }
371	}
372	
373	fn expand_condition(c: &Condition, table: &MacroTable) -> Condition {
374	    match c {
375	        Condition::Eq(x, y) => Condition::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
376	        Condition::Formula(p) => Condition::Formula(expand_formula(p, table)),
377	    }
378	}
379	
380	fn expand_comb(c: &ProcessComb, table: &MacroTable) -> ProcessComb {
381	    match c {
382	        ProcessComb::Parallel => ProcessComb::Parallel,
383	        ProcessComb::Ndc => ProcessComb::Ndc,
384	        ProcessComb::Cond(cond) => ProcessComb::Cond(expand_condition(cond, table)),
385	        ProcessComb::Lookup(t, v) => ProcessComb::Lookup(expand_term(t, table), v.clone()),
386	        ProcessComb::Let { pat, value } => ProcessComb::Let {
387	            pat: expand_term(pat, table),
388	            value: expand_term(value, table),
389	        },
390	    }
391	}
392	
393	fn expand_process(p: &Process, table: &MacroTable) -> Process {
394	    match p {
395	        Process::Null => Process::Null,
396	        Process::Action { action, body } => Process::Action {
397	            action: expand_sapic_action(action, table),
398	            body: Box::new(expand_process(body, table)),
399	        },
400	        Process::Comb { comb, left, right } => Process::Comb {
401	            comb: expand_comb(comb, table),
402	            left: Box::new(expand_process(left, table)),
403	            right: Box::new(expand_process(right, table)),
404	        },
405	        Process::Replication(b) => Process::Replication(Box::new(expand_process(b, table))),
406	        Process::Call { name, args } => Process::Call {
407	            name: name.clone(),
408	            args: args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
409	        },
410	        Process::AtAnnotation(b, t) => {
411	            Process::AtAnnotation(Box::new(expand_process(b, table)), expand_term(t, table))
412	        }
413	    }
414	}
415	
416	fn expand_restriction(r: &Restriction, table: &MacroTable) -> Restriction {
417	    Restriction {
418	        name: r.name.clone(),
419	        formula: expand_formula(&r.formula, table),
420	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
421	    }
422	}
423	
424	fn expand_lemma(l: &Lemma, table: &MacroTable) -> Lemma {
425	    Lemma {
426	        name: l.name.clone(),
427	        modulo: l.modulo.clone(),
428	        attributes: l.attributes.clone(),
429	        trace_quantifier: l.trace_quantifier.clone(),
430	        formula: expand_formula(&l.formula, table),
431	        proof: l.proof.clone(),
432	        plaintext: l.plaintext.clone(),
433	    }
434	}
435	
436	fn expand_predicate(p: &Predicate, table: &MacroTable) -> Predicate {
437	    Predicate {
438	        fact: expand_fact(&p.fact, table),
439	        formula: expand_formula(&p.formula, table),
440	    }
441	}
442	
443	fn expand_item(it: &TheoryItem, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> TheoryItem {
444	    match it {
445	        TheoryItem::Rule(r) => TheoryItem::Rule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
446	        TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => TheoryItem::IntrRule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
447	        TheoryItem::Restriction(r) => TheoryItem::Restriction(expand_restriction(r, table)),
448	        TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(expand_restriction(r, table)),
449	        TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => TheoryItem::Lemma(expand_lemma(l, table)),
450	        // acc-lemma & case-test formulas: full close expands them (the generated
451	        // lemmas' guarded forms show the expansion [Q38,Q39]); the staged view
452	        // leaves them untouched because a later consumer stage owns them [Q41].
453	        TheoryItem::AccLemma(a) => match mode {
454	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::AccLemma(AccLemma {
455	                name: a.name.clone(),
456	                attributes: a.attributes.clone(),
457	                formula: expand_formula(&a.formula, table),
458	                case_test_idents: a.case_test_idents.clone(),
459	            }),
460	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
461	        },
462	        TheoryItem::CaseTest(c) => match mode {
463	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::CaseTest(CaseTest {
464	                name: c.name.clone(),
465	                formula: expand_formula(&c.formula, table),
466	            }),
467	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
468	        },
469	        TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
470	            TheoryItem::Predicates(ps.iter().map(|p| expand_predicate(p, table)).collect())
471	        }
472	        TheoryItem::ProcessDef(pd) => TheoryItem::ProcessDef(ProcessDef {
473	            name: pd.name.clone(),
474	            vars: pd.vars.clone(),
475	            body: expand_process(&pd.body, table),
476	        }),
477	        TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(p) => {
478	            TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(expand_process(p, table))
479	        }
480	        TheoryItem::EquivLemma(a, b) => {
481	            TheoryItem::EquivLemma(expand_process(a, table), expand_process(b, table))
482	        }
483	        TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(p) => {
484	            TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(expand_process(p, table))
485	        }
486	        // Equations carry terms; expand uniformly (a no-op when they contain no
487	        // macro call — not observed in the corpus, but handled for robustness).
488	        TheoryItem::Equations { convergent, eqs } => TheoryItem::Equations {
489	            convergent: *convergent,
490	            eqs: eqs
491	                .iter()
492	                .map(|e| Equation {
493	                    lhs: expand_term(&e.lhs, table),
494	                    rhs: expand_term(&e.rhs, table),
495	                })
496	                .collect(),
497	        },
498	        // Items with no macro-bearing terms are carried through unchanged. The
499	        // `macros:` declaration items pass through here too — retained in place
500	        // with their original, unexpanded bodies [Q37].
501	        TheoryItem::Macros(_)
502	        | TheoryItem::Builtins(_)
503	        | TheoryItem::Functions(_)
504	        | TheoryItem::Options(_)
505	        | TheoryItem::Heuristic(_)
506	        | TheoryItem::Tactic(_)
507	        | TheoryItem::DiffLemma(_)
508	        | TheoryItem::Export { .. }
509	        | TheoryItem::FormalComment { .. }
510	        | TheoryItem::Define(_)
511	        | TheoryItem::Include(_) => it.clone(),
512	    }
513	}
514	
515	#[cfg(test)]
516	mod tests;
517

content.text: The clean macros lib.rs now has both `expand` (FullClose) and `expand_staged` (Staged). Let me read the current repo `macros.rs` and the ported `macro_expand.rs`.

content.content:
1	//! Unit E — `.spthy` macro expansion (clean-room).
2	//!
3	//! `expand(theory)` returns a theory in which every macro use at every use site
4	//! is replaced by its transitively-substituted body, while the `macros:`
5	//! declarations are retained in place (with their original, unexpanded bodies).
6	//! See ../BEHAVIOR.md for the observed semantics this implements; every rule
7	//! below traces to a `[Qn]` observation.
8	//!
9	//! Summary of the semantics implemented here:
10	//!  * a macro `name(f1..fk) = body`; call `name(a1..ak)` binds `fi := ai` and
11	//!    substitutes **simultaneously** (parallel / capture-avoiding)          [Q7]
12	//!  * a bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is a parenthesis-free use
13	//!    of that macro and resolves to its body; `~x`/`$x` and names of arity>=1
14	//!    macros are ordinary variables and do not resolve                      [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]
15	//!  * a formal matches a body variable by **full identity incl. sort**; `~x`
16	//!    and `$x` do NOT match an untagged formal `x` and stay free            [Q27,Q28]
17	//!  * a nullary macro reserves its name against a same-named formal: inside a
18	//!    body the name resolves to the macro, not the formal                   [Q36]
19	//!  * bodies may call only strictly-earlier macros ⇒ the macro dependency
20	//!    graph is a DAG, so expansion always terminates                        [Q8,Q19,Q20]
21	//!  * expansion is transitive: a body's own macro calls are expanded too    [Q9,Q18]
22	//!  * at the AST level a call's arg-count already equals the macro arity
23	//!    (the parser packs/rejects mismatches); `expand` no-ops on a mismatch)  [Q11,Q12,Q15,Q16,Q17]
24	//!  * the `macros:` declaration block is preserved unchanged in the output;
25	//!    the reference retains it in its pretty output and the consuming
26	//!    pipeline requires it in place                                          [Q37]
27	
28	
29	use std::collections::HashMap;
30	use tamarin_parser::ast::*;
31	
32	/// Table of macros whose bodies are already fully expanded (macro-free),
33	/// keyed by macro name. Formals are matched against body variables by full
34	/// `VarSpec` identity, so the substitution map is keyed by `VarSpec`.
35	#[derive(Default)]
36	struct MacroTable {
37	    by_name: HashMap<String, (Vec<VarSpec>, Term)>,
38	}
39	
40	impl MacroTable {
41	    fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&(Vec<VarSpec>, Term)> {
42	        self.by_name.get(name)
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Expand every macro use in `theory`, keeping the `macros:` declarations in
47	/// place.
48	///
49	/// The result, fed back to the oracle, reproduces the reasoning content the
50	/// oracle computes for the original (modulo-AC variants / expanded-formula /
51	/// guarded-formula), which is byte-identical to a hand-inlined equivalent
52	/// (verified by workspace/byteparity.sh and workspace/formula_parity.sh). The
53	/// `macros:` declaration items pass through unchanged (with their original,
54	/// unexpanded bodies): the reference retains the block in its pretty output and
55	/// the consuming pipeline requires it in place [Q37].
56	pub fn expand(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
57	    let table = build_table(theory);
58	    let items = theory.items.iter().map(|it| expand_item(it, &table)).collect();
59	    Theory {
60	        is_diff: theory.is_diff,
61	        name: theory.name.clone(),
62	        configuration: theory.configuration.clone(),
63	        items,
64	    }
65	}
66	
67	/// Build the macro table with fully-expanded (macro-free) bodies.
68	///
69	/// Macros are processed in declaration order; each body is expanded against the
70	/// macros defined strictly before it. Forward/self/mutual references are parse
71	/// errors upstream [Q8,Q19,Q20], so "earlier only" is complete and cannot loop.
72	fn build_table(theory: &Theory) -> MacroTable {
73	    let mut table = MacroTable::default();
74	    for item in &theory.items {
75	        if let TheoryItem::Macros(ms) = item {
76	            for m in ms {
77	                let body = expand_term(&m.body, &table);
78	                table.by_name.insert(m.name.clone(), (m.args.clone(), body));
79	            }
80	        }
81	    }
82	    table
83	}
84	
85	// ---- term-level expansion -------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/// Bottom-up macro expansion of a term. Arguments are expanded first, then a
88	/// macro call is replaced by its body with formals bound to the expanded args.
89	fn expand_term(t: &Term, table: &MacroTable) -> Term {
90	    match t {
91	        // A bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is that macro used
92	        // without parentheses; resolve it to the (already-expanded) body. A
93	        // fresh/pub-sorted name or the name of an arity>=1 macro is an ordinary
94	        // variable and is left untouched [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]. A nullary macro
95	        // reserves its name even against a same-named formal [Q36].
96	        Term::Var(v) => match table.get(&v.name) {
97	            Some((formals, body)) if v.sort == SortHint::Untagged && formals.is_empty() => {
98	                body.clone()
99	            }
100	            _ => t.clone(),
101	        },
102	
103	        Term::PubLit(_)
104	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
105	        | Term::NatLit(_)
106	        | Term::Number(_)
107	        | Term::NumberOne
108	        | Term::NatOne
109	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
110	
111	        Term::App(name, args) => {
112	            let args: Vec<Term> = args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect();
113	            if let Some((formals, body)) = table.get(name) {
114	                // At the AST level arg-count == arity for a valid parse. If it
115	                // ever differs (defensive), leave the call unexpanded.
116	                if formals.len() == args.len() {
117	                    let subst = build_subst(formals, &args);
118	                    // `body` is already macro-free; a simultaneous substitution
119	                    // of macro-free args therefore yields a macro-free term.
120	                    return substitute_term(body, &subst);
121	                }
122	            }
123	            Term::App(name.clone(), args)
124	        }
125	
126	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
127	            name.clone(),
128	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
129	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
130	        ),
131	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| expand_term(x, table)).collect()),
132	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
133	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
134	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
135	        ),
136	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
137	            *op,
138	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
139	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
140	        ),
141	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(expand_term(a, table))),
142	    }
143	}
144	
145	/// Build the simultaneous substitution `formal_i := arg_i`, keyed by the full
146	/// `VarSpec` of each formal (so sort/idx participate in matching) [Q27,Q28].
147	fn build_subst(formals: &[VarSpec], args: &[Term]) -> HashMap<VarSpec, Term> {
148	    formals
149	        .iter()
150	        .cloned()
151	        .zip(args.iter().cloned())
152	        .collect()
153	}
154	
155	/// Apply a simultaneous substitution to `t`. A `Var` that matches a key is
156	/// replaced by the bound term **without** re-descending into it (parallel
157	/// substitution — no capture) [Q7]. Non-matching variables (incl. differently
158	/// sorted `~x`/`$x`) are left untouched [Q27,Q28].
159	fn substitute_term(t: &Term, subst: &HashMap<VarSpec, Term>) -> Term {
160	    match t {
161	        Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(v) {
162	            Some(bound) => bound.clone(),
163	            None => t.clone(),
164	        },
165	        Term::PubLit(_)
166	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
167	        | Term::NatLit(_)
168	        | Term::Number(_)
169	        | Term::NumberOne
170	        | Term::NatOne
171	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
172	        Term::App(name, args) => Term::App(
173	            name.clone(),
174	            args.iter().map(|a| substitute_term(a, subst)).collect(),
175	        ),
176	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
177	            name.clone(),
178	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
179	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
180	        ),
181	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| substitute_term(x, subst)).collect()),
182	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
183	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
184	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
185	        ),
186	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
187	            *op,
188	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
189	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
190	        ),
191	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst))),
192	    }
193	}
194	
195	// ---- structural recursion through the theory ------------------------------
196	
197	fn expand_fact(f: &Fact, table: &MacroTable) -> Fact {
198	    Fact {
199	        persistent: f.persistent,
200	        name: f.name.clone(),
201	        args: f.args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
202	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
203	    }
204	}
205	
206	fn expand_atom(a: &Atom, table: &MacroTable) -> Atom {
207	    match a {
208	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => Atom::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
209	        Atom::Less(x, y) => Atom::Less(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
210	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => Atom::LessMset(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
211	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => Atom::Subterm(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
212	        Atom::Action(f, t) => Atom::Action(expand_fact(f, table), expand_term(t, table)),
213	        Atom::Last(t) => Atom::Last(expand_term(t, table)),
214	        Atom::Pred(f) => Atom::Pred(expand_fact(f, table)),
215	    }
216	}
217	
218	fn expand_formula(phi: &Formula, table: &MacroTable) -> Formula {
219	    match phi {
220	        Formula::False => Formula::False,
221	        Formula::True => Formula::True,
222	        Formula::Atom(a) => Formula::Atom(expand_atom(a, table)),
223	        Formula::Not(p) => Formula::Not(Box::new(expand_formula(p, table))),
224	        Formula::And(p, q) => Formula::And(
225	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
226	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
227	        ),
228	        Formula::Or(p, q) => Formula::Or(
229	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
230	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
231	        ),
232	        Formula::Implies(p, q) => Formula::Implies(
233	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
234	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
235	        ),
236	        Formula::Iff(p, q) => Formula::Iff(
237	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
238	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
239	        ),
240	        Formula::Forall(vs, p) => {
241	            Formula::Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
242	        }
243	        Formula::Exists(vs, p) => {
244	            Formula::Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
245	        }
246	    }
247	}
248	
249	fn expand_let(b: &LetBinding, table: &MacroTable) -> LetBinding {
250	    // A macro call may appear in the let value [Q23]; the bound pattern `var`
251	    // is a variable/pattern and is expanded too for uniformity.
252	    LetBinding {
253	        var: expand_term(&b.var, table),
254	        value: expand_term(&b.value, table),
255	    }
256	}
257	
258	fn expand_rule(r: &Rule, table: &MacroTable) -> Rule {
259	    Rule {
260	        name: r.name.clone(),
261	        modulo: r.modulo.clone(),
262	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
263	        let_block: r.let_block.iter().map(|b| expand_let(b, table)).collect(),
264	        premises: r.premises.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
265	        actions: r.actions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
266	        conclusions: r.conclusions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
267	        embedded_restrictions: r
268	            .embedded_restrictions
269	            .iter()
270	            .map(|p| expand_formula(p, table))
271	            .collect(),
272	        variants: r.variants.iter().map(|v| expand_rule(v, table)).collect(),
273	        left_right: r.left_right.as_ref().map(|(l, rr)| {
274	            (
275	                Box::new(expand_rule(l, table)),
276	                Box::new(expand_rule(rr, table)),
277	            )
278	        }),
279	    }
280	}
281	
282	fn expand_sapic_action(a: &SapicAction, table: &MacroTable) -> SapicAction {
283	    match a {
284	        SapicAction::New(v) => SapicAction::New(v.clone()),
285	        SapicAction::Insert(k, v) => {
286	            SapicAction::Insert(expand_term(k, table), expand_term(v, table))
287	        }
288	        SapicAction::Delete(k) => SapicAction::Delete(expand_term(k, table)),
289	        SapicAction::ChIn { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChIn {
290	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
291	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
292	        },
293	        SapicAction::ChOut { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChOut {
294	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
295	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
296	        },
297	        SapicAction::Lock(t) => SapicAction::Lock(expand_term(t, table)),
298	        SapicAction::Unlock(t) => SapicAction::Unlock(expand_term(t, table)),
299	        SapicAction::Event(f) => SapicAction::Event(expand_fact(f, table)),
300	        SapicAction::Msr { prems, acts, concs, restrictions } => SapicAction::Msr {
301	            prems: prems.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
302	            acts: acts.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
303	            concs: concs.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
304	            restrictions: restrictions.iter().map(|p| expand_formula(p, table)).collect(),
305	        },
306	    }
307	}
308	
309	fn expand_condition(c: &Condition, table: &MacroTable) -> Condition {
310	    match c {
311	        Condition::Eq(x, y) => Condition::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
312	        Condition::Formula(p) => Condition::Formula(expand_formula(p, table)),
313	    }
314	}
315	
316	fn expand_comb(c: &ProcessComb, table: &MacroTable) -> ProcessComb {
317	    match c {
318	        ProcessComb::Parallel => ProcessComb::Parallel,
319	        ProcessComb::Ndc => ProcessComb::Ndc,
320	        ProcessComb::Cond(cond) => ProcessComb::Cond(expand_condition(cond, table)),
321	        ProcessComb::Lookup(t, v) => ProcessComb::Lookup(expand_term(t, table), v.clone()),
322	        ProcessComb::Let { pat, value } => ProcessComb::Let {
323	            pat: expand_term(pat, table),
324	            value: expand_term(value, table),
325	        },
326	    }
327	}
328	
329	fn expand_process(p: &Process, table: &MacroTable) -> Process {
330	    match p {
331	        Process::Null => Process::Null,
332	        Process::Action { action, body } => Process::Action {
333	            action: expand_sapic_action(action, table),
334	            body: Box::new(expand_process(body, table)),
335	        },
336	        Process::Comb { comb, left, right } => Process::Comb {
337	            comb: expand_comb(comb, table),
338	            left: Box::new(expand_process(left, table)),
339	            right: Box::new(expand_process(right, table)),
340	        },
341	        Process::Replication(b) => Process::Replication(Box::new(expand_process(b, table))),
342	        Process::Call { name, args } => Process::Call {
343	            name: name.clone(),
344	            args: args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
345	        },
346	        Process::AtAnnotation(b, t) => {
347	            Process::AtAnnotation(Box::new(expand_process(b, table)), expand_term(t, table))
348	        }
349	    }
350	}
351	
352	fn expand_restriction(r: &Restriction, table: &MacroTable) -> Restriction {
353	    Restriction {
354	        name: r.name.clone(),
355	        formula: expand_formula(&r.formula, table),
356	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
357	    }
358	}
359	
360	fn expand_lemma(l: &Lemma, table: &MacroTable) -> Lemma {
361	    Lemma {
362	        name: l.name.clone(),
363	        modulo: l.modulo.clone(),
364	        attributes: l.attributes.clone(),
365	        trace_quantifier: l.trace_quantifier.clone(),
366	        formula: expand_formula(&l.formula, table),
367	        proof: l.proof.clone(),
368	        plaintext: l.plaintext.clone(),
369	    }
370	}
371	
372	fn expand_predicate(p: &Predicate, table: &MacroTable) -> Predicate {
373	    Predicate {
374	        fact: expand_fact(&p.fact, table),
375	        formula: expand_formula(&p.formula, table),
376	    }
377	}
378	
379	fn expand_item(it: &TheoryItem, table: &MacroTable) -> TheoryItem {
380	    match it {
381	        TheoryItem::Rule(r) => TheoryItem::Rule(expand_rule(r, table)),
382	        TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => TheoryItem::IntrRule(expand_rule(r, table)),
383	        TheoryItem::Restriction(r) => TheoryItem::Restriction(expand_restriction(r, table)),
384	        TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(expand_restriction(r, table)),
385	        TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => TheoryItem::Lemma(expand_lemma(l, table)),
386	        TheoryItem::AccLemma(a) => TheoryItem::AccLemma(AccLemma {
387	            name: a.name.clone(),
388	            attributes: a.attributes.clone(),
389	            formula: expand_formula(&a.formula, table),
390	            case_test_idents: a.case_test_idents.clone(),
391	        }),
392	        TheoryItem::CaseTest(c) => TheoryItem::CaseTest(CaseTest {
393	            name: c.name.clone(),
394	            formula: expand_formula(&c.formula, table),
395	        }),
396	        TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
397	            TheoryItem::Predicates(ps.iter().map(|p| expand_predicate(p, table)).collect())
398	        }
399	        TheoryItem::ProcessDef(pd) => TheoryItem::ProcessDef(ProcessDef {
400	            name: pd.name.clone(),
401	            vars: pd.vars.clone(),
402	            body: expand_process(&pd.body, table),
403	        }),
404	        TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(p) => {
405	            TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(expand_process(p, table))
406	        }
407	        TheoryItem::EquivLemma(a, b) => {
408	            TheoryItem::EquivLemma(expand_process(a, table), expand_process(b, table))
409	        }
410	        TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(p) => {
411	            TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(expand_process(p, table))
412	        }
413	        // Equations carry terms; expand uniformly (a no-op when they contain no
414	        // macro call — not observed in the corpus, but handled for robustness).
415	        TheoryItem::Equations { convergent, eqs } => TheoryItem::Equations {
416	            convergent: *convergent,
417	            eqs: eqs
418	                .iter()
419	                .map(|e| Equation {
420	                    lhs: expand_term(&e.lhs, table),
421	                    rhs: expand_term(&e.rhs, table),
422	                })
423	                .collect(),
424	        },
425	        // Items with no macro-bearing terms are carried through unchanged. Macros
426	        // items are filtered out before this function is called.
427	        TheoryItem::Macros(_)
428	        | TheoryItem::Builtins(_)
429	        | TheoryItem::Functions(_)
430	        | TheoryItem::Options(_)
431	        | TheoryItem::Heuristic(_)
432	        | TheoryItem::Tactic(_)
433	        | TheoryItem::DiffLemma(_)
434	        | TheoryItem::Export { .. }
435	        | TheoryItem::FormalComment { .. }
436	        | TheoryItem::Define(_)
437	        | TheoryItem::Include(_) => it.clone(),
438	    }
439	}
440

content.text: Now let me read the ported `macro_expand.rs` (the file I'll route through and partially delete) and find its callers.

content.content:
1	// Currently GPL 3.0 until granted permission by the following authors:
2	//   jdreier, Hong-Thai, meiersi, rkunnema, ValentinYuri, charlie-j,
3	//   kevinmorio, yavivanov, gilcu3, BTom-GH, racoucho1u, beschmi,
4	//   PhilipLukertWork, rsasse, Nynko, felixlinker, katrielalex, and
5	//   other minor contributors (see upstream git history)
6	// Ported from upstream tamarin-prover sources:
7	//   lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs, lib/theory/src/ClosedTheory.hs,
8	//   lib/theory/src/Items/CaseTestItem.hs, lib/theory/src/Lemma.hs,
9	//   lib/theory/src/Prover.hs, lib/theory/src/Rule.hs,
10	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Fact.hs,
11	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Formula.hs,
12	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Restriction.hs,
13	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Rule.hs,
14	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Sapic/Term.hs,
15	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser.hs,
16	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser/Macro.hs
17	
18	//! Parser-AST level macro expansion.
19	//!
20	//! Port of `Term.Macro.applyMacros` (HS: lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs:40-50)
21	//! plus the call-sites that drive it:
22	//!
23	//!   - `applyMacroInRule`     — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Rule.hs:1032-1037
24	//!   - `applyMacroInFact`     — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Fact.hs:301-303
25	//!   - `applyMacroInFormula`  — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Formula.hs:311-313
26	//!   - `applyMacroInLemma`    — lib/theory/src/Lemma.hs:83-88
27	//!   - `applyMacroInRestriction` — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Restriction.hs:163-165
28	//!   - `closeProtoRule` calls applyMacroInRule BEFORE variantsProtoRule
29	//!     — lib/theory/src/Rule.hs:96-98
30	//!   - `parseLemmaWithMacros`  — lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser.hs:97-105
31	//!
32	//! HS works at the typed `LNTerm` / `LNFact` / `LNFormula` level, with
33	//! macro matching keyed on the `FunSym` (a `NoEq (name, (arity, Private,
34	//! Destructor))` tuple — Macro.hs:30).  RS parses lemma/restriction
35	//! formulas as `parser::ast::Formula` and only converts to `LNFormula`
36	//! later (via `formula_to_guarded`), so the natural place to expand is
37	//! the parser AST.  This is observationally faithful: every macro call
38	//! site is rewritten to its body before either side's typed conversion
39	//! runs.  The macro fun-syms themselves are still registered in MaudeSig
40	//! (HS Parser/Macro.hs:48 `addMacroSym`) so any unexpanded reference —
41	//! and Maude — still see them.
42	//!
43	//! Recursion semantics mirror HS exactly:
44	//!   - args are recursively expanded FIRST (Macro.hs:46),
45	//!   - then substitution into the body,
46	//!   - then the EXPANDED body is recursively re-expanded
47	//!     (Macro.hs:48 `applyMacros macros (apply subst mout)`).
48	//!
49	//! This handles chained / nested macros (e.g. `hashdec` calling `decrypt`
50	//! in `examples/features/macros/MacroExample.spthy`).
51	
52	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
53	
54	use tamarin_parser::ast as p;
55	
56	/// Apply all macros to a term, recursing into args first and re-expanding
57	/// the body after substitution.  Mirrors HS `applyMacros` exactly
58	/// (Term/Macro.hs:40-50).
59	pub fn apply_macros_term(macros: &[p::Macro], term: &p::Term) -> p::Term {
60	    match term {
61	        p::Term::App(name, args) => {
62	            // Recurse on args first (HS `processedArgs = map (applyMacros macros) args`).
63	            let processed_args: Vec<p::Term> =
64	                args.iter().map(|a| apply_macros_term(macros, a)).collect();
65	            // Match on (name, arity) — HS matches on FunSym which includes
66	            // arity (Macro.hs:30 `macroToFunSym (op,args,_) = NoEq (op,
67	            // (length args, Private, Destructor))` ; matching macros are
68	            // found via Macro.hs:53-54 `find (\m -> macroToFunSym m == f)`).
69	            if let Some(m) = find_matching_macro(name, processed_args.len(), macros) {
70	                // Build the param→arg substitution (by name).
71	                let mut subst: BTreeMap<String, p::Term> = BTreeMap::new();
72	                for (param, value) in m.args.iter().zip(processed_args.iter()) {
73	                    subst.insert(param.name.clone(), value.clone());
74	                }
75	                let expanded = subst_term_by_name(&m.body, &subst);
76	                // Re-expand the EXPANDED body to handle nested macros.
77	                apply_macros_term(macros, &expanded)
78	            } else {
79	                p::Term::App(name.clone(), processed_args)
80	            }
81	        }
82	        p::Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => p::Term::AlgApp(
83	            name.clone(),
84	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
85	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
86	        ),
87	        p::Term::Pair(items) => p::Term::Pair(
88	            items.iter().map(|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t)).collect(),
89	        ),
90	        p::Term::Diff(a, b) => p::Term::Diff(
91	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
92	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
93	        ),
94	        p::Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => p::Term::BinOp(
95	            *op,
96	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
97	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
98	        ),
99	        p::Term::PatMatch(inner) => p::Term::PatMatch(
100	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, inner)),
101	        ),
102	        // A BARE identifier (no `$~#%` prefix, no `:sort` suffix, no `.idx`)
103	        // naming a 0-ary macro is a macro CALL: HS's `nullaryApp` parser
104	        // alternative (Term.hs:143-148) runs before `plit` and matches any
105	        // arity-0 name in `funSyms ∪ macroNames`, so such an identifier
106	        // reaches HS's `applyMacros` as `fApp (NoEq (m,(0,..))) []`, never
107	        // as a variable.  RS's surface parser is signature-less and yields
108	        // `Var`, so resolve here.  Any sort/index decoration means HS's
109	        // `symbol` match would have left trailing input and backtracked to
110	        // `plit` — a genuine variable; leave those (and 0-ary FUNCTION
111	        // names, which `term_to_lnterm`'s USER_NULLARY_FUNS branch lifts)
112	        // untouched.
113	        p::Term::Var(v) => {
114	            if v.idx == 0 && v.sort == p::SortHint::Untagged && v.typ.is_none() {
115	                if let Some(m) = find_matching_macro(&v.name, 0, macros) {
116	                    let expanded = subst_term_by_name(&m.body, &BTreeMap::new());
117	                    // Re-expand the EXPANDED body to handle nested macros.
118	                    return apply_macros_term(macros, &expanded);
119	                }
120	            }
121	            term.clone()
122	        }
123	        // Literals: no recursion (HS Macro.hs:51 `Lit l -> lit l`).
124	        p::Term::PubLit(_) | p::Term::FreshLit(_)
125	        | p::Term::NatLit(_) | p::Term::Number(_) | p::Term::NumberOne
126	        | p::Term::NatOne | p::Term::DhNeutral => term.clone(),
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	/// HS `findMatchingMacro f macros = find (\m -> macroToFunSym m == f)`
131	/// (Macro.hs:53-54).  At parser-AST level a call-site has no FunSym
132	/// flags so we match by (name, arity) — equivalent for non-clashing
133	/// macro names since `addMacroSym` rejects redefinitions and built-in
134	/// fun-syms have known fixed arity (parser/Macro.hs:45-49 `case lookup
135	/// op (stFunSyms ++ macroNames) -> fail`).
136	fn find_matching_macro<'a>(
137	    name: &str,
138	    arity: usize,
139	    macros: &'a [p::Macro],
140	) -> Option<&'a p::Macro> {
141	    macros.iter().find(|m| m.name == name && m.args.len() == arity)
142	}
143	
144	/// Apply a name-keyed substitution to a parser term.  HS's typed
145	/// `apply subst term` (Macro.hs:48) becomes a structural name-keyed walk.
146	/// Shared with `predicate_expand` (whose `Subst` newtype wraps the same
147	/// `BTreeMap<String, p::Term>`), so both stay in lockstep on capture /
148	/// replacement semantics.
149	pub(crate) fn subst_term_by_name(t: &p::Term, subst: &BTreeMap<String, p::Term>) -> p::Term {
150	    match t {
151	        p::Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(&v.name) {
152	            Some(replacement) => replacement.clone(),
153	            None => t.clone(),
154	        },
155	        p::Term::App(name, args) => p::Term::App(
156	            name.clone(),
157	            args.iter().map(|a| subst_term_by_name(a, subst)).collect(),
158	        ),
159	        p::Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => p::Term::AlgApp(
160	            name.clone(),
161	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
162	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
163	        ),
164	        p::Term::Pair(items) => p::Term::Pair(
165	            items.iter().map(|a| subst_term_by_name(a, subst)).collect(),
166	        ),
167	        p::Term::Diff(a, b) => p::Term::Diff(
168	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
169	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
170	        ),
171	        p::Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => p::Term::BinOp(
172	            *op,
173	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
174	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
175	        ),
176	        p::Term::PatMatch(inner) => p::Term::PatMatch(
177	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(inner, subst)),
178	        ),
179	        other => other.clone(),
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild a fact, mapping `g` over every arg.
184	/// The single traversal shape behind `apply_macros_fact`,
185	/// `elaborate::canonicalize_ac_in_pfact`, and `elaborate::rewrite_arity1_fact`
186	/// (each supplies its own leaf `&Term -> Term`).
187	pub(crate) fn map_fact_terms(f: &p::Fact, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Fact {
188	    p::Fact {
189	        persistent: f.persistent,
190	        name: f.name.clone(),
191	        args: f.args.iter().map(g).collect(),
192	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
193	    }
194	}
195	
196	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild an atom, mapping `g` over every term and
197	/// `map_fact_terms` over embedded facts.  See [`map_fact_terms`].
198	pub(crate) fn map_atom_terms(a: &p::Atom, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Atom {
199	    use p::Atom::*;
200	    match a {
201	        Eq(x, y) => Eq(g(x), g(y)),
202	        Less(x, y) => Less(g(x), g(y)),
203	        LessMset(x, y) => LessMset(g(x), g(y)),
204	        Subterm(x, y) => Subterm(g(x), g(y)),
205	        Action(f, t) => Action(map_fact_terms(f, g), g(t)),
206	        Last(t) => Last(g(t)),
207	        Pred(f) => Pred(map_fact_terms(f, g)),
208	    }
209	}
210	
211	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild a formula, mapping `g` over every leaf
212	/// term while cloning quantifier `VarSpec`s unchanged.  See [`map_fact_terms`].
213	pub(crate) fn map_formula_terms(f: &p::Formula, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Formula {
214	    use p::Formula::*;
215	    match f {
216	        False => False,
217	        True => True,
218	        Atom(a) => Atom(map_atom_terms(a, g)),
219	        Not(x) => Not(Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
220	        And(x, y) => And(
221	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
222	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
223	        Or(x, y) => Or(
224	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
225	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
226	        Implies(x, y) => Implies(
227	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
228	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
229	        Iff(x, y) => Iff(
230	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
231	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
232	        Forall(vs, x) => Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
233	        Exists(vs, x) => Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
234	    }
235	}
236	
237	/// Apply macros to every term in a fact.  Mirrors HS `applyMacroInFact`
238	/// (Fact.hs:301-303 `applyMacroInFact mcs (Fact tag annot terms) =
239	/// Fact tag annot (map (applyMacros mcs) terms)`).
240	pub fn apply_macros_fact(macros: &[p::Macro], f: &p::Fact) -> p::Fact {
241	    map_fact_terms(f, &|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t))
242	}
243	
244	/// Apply macros to every term in a formula.  Mirrors HS
245	/// `applyMacroInFormula` (Formula.hs:311-313) — `mapAtoms (... applyMacros
246	/// (lnMacrosToBNMacros macros))`.  In RS, parser-AST quantifiers carry
247	/// `VarSpec`s with names; macro params have their declared names; the
248	/// substitution-by-name suffices because the body is closed over the
249	/// param names and call args at every call site, so no quantifier-bound
250	/// variable in the surrounding formula can ever be a param (the macro
251	/// definition is independent of the use site).
252	pub fn apply_macros_formula(macros: &[p::Macro], f: &p::Formula) -> p::Formula {
253	    map_formula_terms(f, &|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t))
254	}
255	
256	/// Apply macros to all items in a theory.  Mirrors HS's call-sites:
257	///   - rule prems/concs/acts (Rule.hs:1032-1037 + ClosedTheory.hs:322-323)
258	///   - lemma formula (Lemma.hs:83-88, called from Parser.hs:105)
259	///   - restriction formula (Restriction.hs:163-165)
260	///   - embedded restriction in rule (treat as formula)
261	///   - rule let-block RHS (already inlined into the rule by
262	///     `apply_let_block` at elaborate time)
263	///
264	/// Macros are collected from `TheoryItem::Macros` items in the theory.
265	/// If no macros are declared, the theory is left unchanged (HS:
266	/// `applyMacroInFormula [] fm = fm`).
267	pub fn expand_theory_macros(thy: &mut p::Theory) {
268	    let macros: Vec<p::Macro> = thy.items.iter().filter_map(|i| match i {
269	        p::TheoryItem::Macros(ms) => Some(ms.clone()),
270	        _ => None,
271	    }).flatten().collect();
272	
273	    if macros.is_empty() { return; }
274	
275	    expand_items(&macros, &mut thy.items);
276	}
277	
278	/// Clone a parser theory and expand its macros, mirroring HS `thyProtoRules`'s
279	/// `applyMacroInRule (theoryMacros thy)`.  Used for the WF re-checks (batch and
280	/// web load paths) that must see macro-expanded rules.
281	pub fn macro_expanded_clone(parsed: &p::Theory) -> p::Theory {
282	    let mut t = parsed.clone();
283	    expand_theory_macros(&mut t);
284	    t
285	}
286	
287	/// Apply macros to a slice of theory items.  The parser splices `#ifdef`
288	/// live branches into the top-level stream (like HS's parse-time
289	/// preprocessing), so a plain walk sees every macro call-site.
290	fn expand_items(macros: &[p::Macro], items: &mut [p::TheoryItem]) {
291	    for item in items.iter_mut() {
292	        match item {
293	            p::TheoryItem::Rule(r) | p::TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => {
294	                expand_rule(macros, r);
295	            }
296	            p::TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => {
297	                l.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &l.formula);
298	            }
299	            p::TheoryItem::Restriction(r) | p::TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => {
300	                r.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &r.formula);
301	            }
302	            // CaseTest / AccLemma are `TranslationItem`s in HS, which
303	            // `closeTheoryItem` passes through verbatim with NO macro
304	            // application (Prover.hs:204 `TranslationItem`; added unmacroed
305	            // via Parser.hs:157,163 `liftedAddAccLemma`/`liftedAddCaseTest`).
306	            // They stay `SyntacticLNFormula` and are only `toLNFormula`'d
307	            // during accountability translation (Items/CaseTestItem.hs:34-37),
308	            // which does not run macros. So we deliberately do NOT expand them
309	            // (they fall into the `_ => {}` arm below).
310	            //
311	            // Predicates: bodies are themselves formula templates. Apply
312	            // macros so a predicate body that calls a macro is expanded
313	            // before predicate-expand inlines it.
314	            p::TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
315	                for pred in ps.iter_mut() {
316	                    pred.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &pred.formula);
317	                    pred.fact = apply_macros_fact(macros, &pred.fact);
318	                }
319	            }
320	            _ => {}
321	        }
322	    }
323	}
324	
325	fn expand_rule(macros: &[p::Macro], r: &mut p::Rule) {
326	    for f in &mut r.premises { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
327	    for f in &mut r.actions { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
328	    for f in &mut r.conclusions { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
329	    for phi in &mut r.embedded_restrictions {
330	        *phi = apply_macros_formula(macros, phi);
331	    }
332	    // Let-block: macros can appear on the RHS.  apply_let_block (in
333	    // elaborate.rs) substitutes these into the body after parsing; we
334	    // expand on the LHS-and-RHS terms here so a let `x = macro(...)`
335	    // sees its RHS rewritten before `apply_let_block` substitutes it.
336	    for b in &mut r.let_block {
337	        b.value = apply_macros_term(macros, &b.value);
338	        b.var = apply_macros_term(macros, &b.var);
339	    }
340	    // Variants / diff sides are passed through UNCHANGED, matching HS.
341	    // `variants` is the user-written explicit `variants ...` block (HS
342	    // OpenProtoRule's ruAC) and `left_right` is the diff `left ... right ...`
343	    // block (HS DiffProtoRule's sides). `applyMacroInProtoRule` /
344	    // `applyMacroInDiffProtoRule` (ClosedTheory.hs:319,323) only run
345	    // applyMacroInRule on the main rule `ruE` and leave variants/sides intact,
346	    // so a macro call inside an explicit variant must survive unexpanded.
347	}
348

355	        parse_theory(src, &[]).expect("parse")

358	    #[test]
359	    fn bare_nullary_macro_name_expands() {
360	        // HS `nullaryApp` (Term.hs:143-148) parses a BARE arity-0 macro
361	        // name as a 0-ary application, so `konst` and `konst()` are the
362	        // same call.  A sorted/indexed variable of the same name is NOT
363	        // a call.
364	        let src = "theory T begin\n\
365	            builtins: hashing\n\
366	            macros: konst() = h('seed')\n\
367	            rule R: [ In(konst) ] --[ M(konst.1, konst:pub) ]-> [ ]\n\
368	            end\n";
369	        let mut thy = parse(src);
370	        expand_theory_macros(&mut thy);
371	        let rule = thy.items.iter().find_map(|i| match i {
372	            p::TheoryItem::Rule(r) => Some(r),
373	            _ => None,
374	        }).unwrap();
375	        // Premise In(konst) → In(h('seed')).
376	        assert!(matches!(&rule.premises[0].args[0],
377	            p::Term::App(n, args) if n == "h" && args.len() == 1),
378	            "got {:?}", rule.premises[0].args[0]);
379	        // konst.1 (indexed) and konst:pub (sorted) stay variables.
380	        assert!(matches!(&rule.actions[0].args[0],
381	            p::Term::Var(v) if v.name == "konst" && v.idx == 1),
382	            "got {:?}", rule.actions[0].args[0]);
383	        assert!(matches!(&rule.actions[0].args[1],
384	            p::Term::Var(v) if v.name == "konst"
385	                && v.sort != p::SortHint::Untagged),
386	            "got {:?}", rule.actions[0].args[1]);
387	    }

395	            end\n";

399	            p::TheoryItem::Rule(r) => Some(r),
400	            _ => None,

403	        let arg = &rule.premises[0].args[0];

409	        // hashdec(x, y) = h(decrypt(x, y)); decrypt(x, y) = adec(x, y).
410	        // Expanding hashdec(a, b) should produce h(adec(a, b)).
411	        let src = "theory T begin\n\
412	            builtins: hashing, asymmetric-encryption\n\
413	            macros: decrypt(x, y) = adec(x, y), hashdec(x, y) = h(decrypt(x, y))\n\
414	            rule R: [ In(hashdec(a, b)) ] --> [ ]\n\
415	            end\n";
416	        let mut thy = parse(src);

423	        // Expected: App("h", [App("adec", [Var(a), Var(b)])])
424	        if let p::Term::App(h_name, h_args) = arg {
425	            assert_eq!(h_name, "h");

509	    #[test]
510	    fn case_test_formula_is_not_macro_expanded() {
511	        // HS keeps CaseTest as a `TranslationItem` and applies NO macros to
512	        // it (Prover.hs:204; Parser.hs:163 `liftedAddCaseTest`). Probed
513	        // against the real HS prover (v1.13.0) on an equivalent theory: the
514	        // stored case-test formula prints `Blame( idm(a) )` UNEXPANDED, e.g.
515	        //   predicate: Blamed( a ) <=> ∃ #i. Blame( idm(a) ) @ #i
516	        // So after `expand_theory_macros` the case-test formula must still
517	        // contain the macro call `App("idm", ...)`.
518	        let src = "theory T begin\n\
519	            functions: id/1\n\
520	            macros: idm(x) = id(x)\n\
521	            rule R: [ In(x) ] --[ Blame(x) ]-> [ Out(x) ]\n\
522	            test blamed: \"Ex #i. Blame(idm(a)) @ #i\"\n\
523	            end\n";
524	        let mut thy = parse(src);
525	        expand_theory_macros(&mut thy);
526	        let ct = thy.items.iter().find_map(|i| match i {
527	            p::TheoryItem::CaseTest(c) => Some(c),
528	            _ => None,
529	        }).expect("case test");
530	        // The Action atom's fact arg must remain App("idm", [Var("a")]).
531	        fn check(f: &p::Formula) -> bool {
532	            match f {
533	                p::Formula::Exists(_, body) | p::Formula::Forall(_, body) => check(body),
534	                p::Formula::And(a, b) | p::Formula::Or(a, b)
535	                | p::Formula::Implies(a, b) | p::Formula::Iff(a, b) => check(a) || check(b),
536	                p::Formula::Not(g) => check(g),
537	                p::Formula::Atom(p::Atom::Action(fact, _)) => {
538	                    matches!(fact.args.first(),
539	                        Some(p::Term::App(name, args)) if name == "idm" && args.len() == 1)
540	                }
541	                _ => false,
542	            }
543	        }
544	        assert!(check(&ct.formula),
545	            "case-test formula was macro-expanded (idm should survive): {:?}",
546	            ct.formula);
547	    }

551	        // AccLemma is also a `TranslationItem` (Prover.hs:204; Parser.hs:157
552	        // `liftedAddAccLemma`) and is never macro-expanded. After
553	        // `expand_theory_macros` the acc-lemma formula must still contain the
554	        // macro call `App("idm", ...)`.
555	        let src = "theory T begin\n\
556	            functions: id/1\n\
557	            macros: idm(x) = id(x)\n\
558	            rule R: [ In(x) ] --[ Blame(x), Fin() ]-> [ Out(x) ]\n\
559	            test blamed: \"Ex #i. Blame(idm(a)) @ #i\"\n\
560	            lemma acc: blamed accounts for \"All #i. Fin() @ #i ==> Ex #j. Blame(idm(a)) @ #j\"\n\
561	            end\n";
562	        let mut thy = parse(src);
563	        expand_theory_macros(&mut thy);
564	        let acc = thy.items.iter().find_map(|i| match i {
565	            p::TheoryItem::AccLemma(a) => Some(a),
566	            _ => None,
567	        }).expect("acc lemma");
568	        fn check(f: &p::Formula) -> bool {
569	            match f {
570	                p::Formula::Exists(_, body) | p::Formula::Forall(_, body) => check(body),
571	                p::Formula::And(a, b) | p::Formula::Or(a, b)
572	                | p::Formula::Implies(a, b) | p::Formula::Iff(a, b) => check(a) || check(b),
573	                p::Formula::Not(g) => check(g),
574	                p::Formula::Atom(p::Atom::Action(fact, _)) => {
575	                    matches!(fact.args.first(),
576	                        Some(p::Term::App(name, args)) if name == "idm" && args.len() == 1)
577	                }
578	                _ => false,
579	            }
580	        }
581	        assert!(check(&acc.formula),
582	            "acc-lemma formula was macro-expanded (idm should survive): {:?}",
583	            acc.formula);
584	    }
585	
586	    #[test]
587	    fn no_macro_means_no_change() {
588	        let src = "theory T begin\n\
589	            rule R: [ In(a) ] --> [ ]\n\
590	            end\n";
591	        let mut thy = parse(src);
592	        let before = thy.clone();
593	        expand_theory_macros(&mut thy);
594	        assert_eq!(thy, before);
595	    }
596	}

content.text: Let me examine the clean crate's own tests, its BEHAVIOR notes on bare-nullary handling, and the parser AST's VarSpec/SortHint to understand a potential idx-handling difference.

The clean tests only ever build `VarSpec` with `idx: 0` — they never exercise the `konst.1` (indexed) case that the captured test `bare_nullary_macro_name_expands` asserts stays a Var. The clean `expand_term` Var arm checks only `v.sort == SortHint::Untagged && formals.is_empty()`, ignoring `idx`/`typ`, whereas the ported code requires `v.idx == 0 && ... && v.typ.is_none()`. This is a potential gate-breaker. Let me verify how the parser represents `konst.1` and `konst:pub`.

content.text:
`konst:pub` parses to `Suffix(Pub)` (not `Untagged`), so the clean expander correctly leaves it. But `konst.1` parses to `sort: Untagged, idx: 1`, and the clean `expand_term` Var arm ignores `idx`, so it would over-expand it — where the captured test asserts it stays a Var. I need to verify this empirically. Let me first re-sync `macros.rs` from the round-5 workspace, then wire and test.

input.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
src = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/macros/workspace/macro-clean/src/lib.rs"
dst = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs"
with open(src) as f:
    text = f.read()

lines = text.split("\n")
out = []
i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    line = lines[i]
    if line == "pub mod ast;":
        # drop this line and, if the following line is blank, keep structure sane
        i += 1
        continue
    if line == "use ast::*;":
        out.append("use tamarin_parser::ast::*;")
        i += 1
        continue
    if line == "#[cfg(test)]" and i+1 < len(lines) and lines[i+1] == "mod tests;":
        # drop these two lines and any trailing blank line before them
        # remove a preceding blank line if present
        if out and out[-1] == "":
            out.pop()
        i += 2
        continue
    out.append(line)
    i += 1

new_text = "\n".join(out)
with open(dst, "w") as f:
    f.write(new_text)
print("written", len(new_text), "bytes")

20	//! Port of Haskell's `Theory.Constraint.System.Dot` +
21	//! `Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.*` — convert a `System` into a
22	//! Graphviz DOT representation suitable for `dot -Tsvg`.
23	//!
24	//! We render the same kinds of nodes / edges / clusters as a single
25	//! self-contained DOT document, including an HTML-table legend for the
26	//! chosen abbreviations and similar-name / role clustering. Node ids,
27	//! fact rendering (`prettyLNFact`), action-row filtering (Diff /
28	//! auto-source), the cluster/preamble attribute blocks, the `roleColor`
29	//! cluster styling and the less-edge rendering all match HS byte-for-
30	//! byte.
31	//!
32	//! Per-rule node FILL colours are a faithful port of HS `nodeColorMap`
33	//! (Dot.hs:190-218): the size-dependent light-HSV palette keyed by
34	//! `(groupIdx, memberIdx)` — see `build_node_color_map` / `NodeColorMap`
35	//! below. An explicit per-rule `color:` attribute and a cluster's
36	//! `manualNodeColor` still take priority (HS `dotNodeCompact`, Dot.hs:248-256).
37	//! Each rule record also carries HS's `fontcolor` (`colorUsesWhiteFont` of the
38	//! palette colour, Dot.hs:258/284-287) and `role` (Dot.hs:259) attributes.
39	//!
40	//! KNOWN DIVERGENCES:
41	//!   * (serialization form only — normalised away by the parse-and-compare
42	//!     gate) the cluster subgraph identifier uses the Rust `cluster_<n>` form
43	//!     rather than HS `createClusterNodeId roleName`. The cluster's label /
44	//!     colour / membership are all faithful.
45	//!   * HS `mkNode`'s `CompactBoringNodes` branch (Dot.hs:294-304) — PORTED
46	//!     (see `rule_node`): under the default node style, intruder rules and the
47	//!     `Fresh` rule collapse to a PLAIN `mkSimpleNode` ellipse (Dot.hs:289-290)
48	//!     with no fill/font/role attrs. The label is `show v : showDotRuleCaseName
49	//!     ru` when the node has an outgoing edge (`hasOutgoingEdge`, Dot.hs:277-279,
50	//!     over the TOP-LEVEL `grEdges` only), else the full rule label incl. the
51	//!     bracketed action row. The `uncompact`/`FullBoringNodes` toggle is not
52	//!     plumbed through the RS handler (see `graph/options.rs`), so this route is
53	//!     always compact — matching the HS default (`defaultDotOptions`, Dot.hs:82).
54	//!   * SERIALIZATION form only (normalised away by the parse-and-compare gate):
55	//!     protocol-rule RECORD labels use RS port ids `<p0>`/`<c0>` and spaced
56	//!     `{ .. } | .. | { .. }` bracketing, where HS's `Text.Dot.renderRecord`
57	//!     (Dot.hs:254-280) uses a graph-global port counter `<n0>`, `<n1>`, … and
58	//!     `{{..|..}|{..}|{..|..}}` bracketing. The gate ignores the node-id scheme
59	//!     and record bracketing; the field CONTENT (facts, `id : name[acts]`) is
60	//!     rendered identically.
61	//!
62	//! Reference:
63	//!   - `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs` (605 lines)
64	//!   - `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Graph.hs`
65	//!   - `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/GraphRepr.hs`
66	//!
67	//! The shape mirrors `systemToGraph` + `dotSystemCompact`:
68	//!   1. Collect nodes from `sNodes`, plus "missing" nodes referenced
69	//!      by edges but absent from `sNodes`.
70	//!   2. Add unsolved-action-atom nodes (KU goals at fresh ids).
71	//!   3. Add the `LastAtom` node, if any.
72	//!   4. Emit edges from `sEdges` (conclusion → premise) styled by
73	//!      fact tag.
74	//!   5. Emit less-edges from `sLessAtoms` (dashed, coloured by
75	//!      reason).
76	//!   6. Emit chain edges from unsolved Chain goals (dotted green).
77	//!
78	//! Each rule node is rendered as a Graphviz record:
79	//!
80	//! ```text
81	//!     +------------+------------+
82	//!     |  prem_0    |  prem_1    |
83	//!     +------------+------------+
84	//!     |     <#i> : RuleName     |
85	//!     +------------+------------+
86	//!     |  conc_0    |  conc_1    |
87	//!     +------------+------------+
88	//! ```
89	//!
90	//! with port names `p0`, `p1`, ..., `c0`, `c1`, ... so that edges from
91	//! the `sEdges` set can target the correct slots.
92	
93	// every `HashMap`/`HashSet` in this DOT renderer is a
94	// keyed-lookup / membership helper — `node_map` (node id -> rule, `.get`),
95	// `has_outgoing` / `port_owner_ids` / `used_dot_ids` (`.contains` / `.insert`
96	// dedup).  DOT output ORDER is driven by iterating the ordered
97	// `repr.nodes` / `repr.edges` Vecs; these maps/sets are never iterated into
98	// output.  Also off the batch `--prove` byte-parity surface (server graph UI).
99	// std kept (byte-inert).
100	#![allow(clippy::disallowed_types)]
101	
102	use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
103	use std::fmt::Write as _;
104	
105	use tamarin_theory::constraint::constraints::{LessAtom, NodeId, Reason};
106	use tamarin_theory::constraint::system::System;
107	use tamarin_theory::fact::{FactTag, LNFact};
108	use tamarin_theory::pretty_hpj::{self, Doc, WEB_LINE_LENGTH, WEB_RIBBON};
109	use tamarin_theory::rule::{
110	    rule_name_string, IntrRuleACInfo, ProtoRuleName, RuleACInst, RuleInfo,
111	};
112	use tamarin_term::lterm::{LNTerm, LVar};
113	use tamarin_term::pretty::pretty_lnterm;
114	// `fix_multi_line_label` is HS `fixMultiLineLabel` (Text/Dot.hs:355-363),
115	// applied to every record FIELD by the `mkField` smart constructor
116	// (Text/Dot.hs:378-381): a multi-line label has each line's leading spaces
117	// replaced 1:1 by ` ` and is re-joined with `unlines` — which appends a
118	// TRAILING newline (→ a trailing `\l` after `showAttr`).  Single-line labels
119	// pass through untouched.
120	use tamarin_utils::dot::fix_multi_line_label;
121	
122	use crate::graph::abbreviation::{
123	    apply_abbreviations_fact, compute_abbreviations, AbbreviationOptions,
124	    Abbreviations,
125	};
126	use crate::graph::options::GraphOptions;
127	use crate::graph::repr::{
128	    add_cluster_by_role, add_intelligent_cluster_using_similar_names,
129	    compute_basic_graph_repr, extract_base_name, extract_role, GEdge, GNode,
130	    MissingHint, NodeType,
131	};
132	use crate::graph::simplify::{compress_system, simplify_system};
133	use crate::graph::render_system::RenderSystem;
