135	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
136	// Public API
137	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
138	
139	/// Render a [`System`] into a Graphviz DOT document with default
140	/// graph options (Haskell `defaultGraphOptions`: SL2 + compress).
141	/// Returns a self-contained `digraph G { ... }` block.
142	pub fn system_to_dot(sys: &System) -> String {
143	    system_to_dot_with(sys, &GraphOptions::default())
144	}
145	
146	/// Render a [`System`] into a Graphviz DOT document under the given
147	/// options.  Applies compression, simplification, role-clustering, and
148	/// abbreviation discovery before emitting DOT, mirroring Haskell's
149	/// `systemToGraph` + `dotSystemCompact`.
150	pub fn system_to_dot_with(sys: &System, opts: &GraphOptions) -> String {
151	    // 1. Pre-render simplification.  Clone-for-render boundary: from here on the
152	    //    working copy is a `RenderSystem` (display-only, write-sealed) so it can
153	    //    never be fed back into the prover — the compress/simplify passes mutate
154	    //    it in ways that leave the `subst_system` stamps meaningless.
155	    let working = RenderSystem::from_prover(sys.clone());
156	    let working = if opts.compress { compress_system(working) } else { working };
157	    let working = simplify_system(opts.simplification_level, working);
158	    // 2. Build the GraphRepr.  `compute_basic_graph_repr` takes `&System`;
159	    //    `&RenderSystem` derefs to it.
160	    let mut repr = compute_basic_graph_repr(&working);
161	    if opts.clustering_similar_names {
162	        add_intelligent_cluster_using_similar_names(&mut repr);
163	    } else {
164	        add_cluster_by_role(&mut repr);
165	    }
166	    // 3. Compute abbreviations.
167	    let abbrevs: Abbreviations = if opts.abbreviate {
168	        compute_abbreviations(&repr, &AbbreviationOptions::default())
169	    } else {
170	        Abbreviations::new()
171	    };
172	    // 4. Emit DOT.
173	    let mut g = DotBuilder::new();
174	    // HS `dotSystemCompact` (Dot.hs:481-487) computes the node colour map from
175	    // the RAW system's nodes (`nodeColorMap (M.elems $ get sNodes se)`), NOT
176	    // the compressed/simplified `working` used for the graph. Mirror that: the
177	    // palette is sized by the whole rule set, so it must see every original
178	    // node.
179	    let color_map = build_node_color_map(&sys.nodes);
180	    // HS `dotGraphCompact` (Dot.hs:503) switches the graph-level defaults to
181	    // `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` when the repr has any clusters.
182	    g.preamble(!repr.clusters.is_empty());
183	    let abbrev_lookup = |t: &LNTerm| -> Option<LNTerm> {
184	        abbrevs.get(t).map(|(a, _)| a.clone())
185	    };
186	    // Precompute a node-id -> rule map so edge styling is O(1) per edge
187	    // instead of scanning `working.nodes` per edge.
188	    let node_map: HashMap<&LVar, &RuleACInst> =
189	        working.nodes.iter().map(|(id, ru)| (id, ru)).collect();
190	    // HS `hasOutgoingEdge graph v` (Dot.hs:277-279): a node has an outgoing edge
191	    // iff it is the conclusion-side source of some `SystemEdge` in the graph's
192	    // TOP-LEVEL edge set (`get grEdges repr`). Clustering removes a cluster's
193	    // internal edges from `grEdges` (GraphRepr.hs:126-129), so we mirror HS and
194	    // consult ONLY `repr.edges` (post-clustering), never a cluster's own edges.
195	    // Drives the compact-node label choice in `rule_node`.
196	    let has_outgoing: HashSet<&LVar> = repr.edges.iter()
197	        .filter_map(|e| match e {
198	            GEdge::System(src, _) => Some(&src.0),
199	            _ => None,
200	        })
201	        .collect();
202	    // HS gives every node a globally-fresh dot id via `cacheState dsNodes`
203	    // (Dot.hs:108-110), so a single system-node id `v` that is ALSO an
204	    // unsolved-action atom and/or the last-action atom is drawn as SEVERAL
205	    // distinct dot nodes (`n5` record + `n7` ellipse …). RS's semantic dot-id
206	    // scheme derives one id per `v` (`dot_node_id`), so those extra ellipses
207	    // would collide with the record on a single id — graphviz merges them (the
208	    // ellipse overwrites the record) and the parity gate's label-keyed node map
209	    // drops the record. Mirror HS: the SystemNode / MissingNode own the base id
210	    // (their record ports / bare id are what `sEdges` reference via
211	    // `conc_port_ref`/`prem_port_ref` = HS `dsConcs`/`dsPrems`), and a colliding
212	    // UnsolvedAction / LastAction ellipse (which HS never references by an
213	    // sEdge) gets a distinct suffixed id.
214	    //
215	    // `dsNodes[v]` (which HS `dotLessEdge` resolves each less-edge endpoint
216	    // through, Dot.hs:408-409) is the LAST dot node emitted at `v`. Emission
217	    // order is the free `repr.nodes` (System, UnsolvedAction, LastAction,
218	    // Missing per `compute_basic_graph_repr`) then each cluster's nodes — so we
219	    // walk that exact order, assigning ids and overwriting `ds_nodes` as we go.
220	    //
221	    // `port_owner_ids` = the `v`s whose base id backs an sEdge port ref
222	    // (SystemNode records + MissingNodes). An UnsolvedAction/LastAction at such
223	    // a `v` MUST yield the base to that owner regardless of emission order (a
224	    // SystemNode can be clustered and thus emitted AFTER a free action ellipse).
225	    let port_owner_ids: HashSet<&LVar> = repr.nodes.iter()
226	        .chain(repr.clusters.iter().flat_map(|c| c.nodes.iter()))
227	        .filter(|n| matches!(n.ty, NodeType::System(_) | NodeType::Missing(_)))
228	        .map(|n| &n.id)
229	        .collect();
230	    let mut used_dot_ids: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
231	    // Assigned id for each UnsolvedAction (tag 0) / LastAction (tag 1) node,
232	    // keyed by (node id, tag) — at most one of each kind per `v`.
233	    let mut ellipse_dot_ids: std::collections::BTreeMap<(LVar, u8), String> =
234	        std::collections::BTreeMap::new();
235	    // `dsNodes`: v -> dot id of the LAST node emitted at v (less-edge target).
236	    let mut ds_nodes: std::collections::BTreeMap<LVar, String> =
237	        std::collections::BTreeMap::new();
238	    for node in repr.nodes.iter()
239	        .chain(repr.clusters.iter().flat_map(|c| c.nodes.iter()))
240	    {
241	        let base = DotBuilder::dot_node_id(&node.id);
242	        let id = match &node.ty {
243	            // Base-id owners: their id is referenced by sEdge port refs.
244	            NodeType::System(_) | NodeType::Missing(_) => {
245	                used_dot_ids.insert(base.clone());
246	                base
247	            }
248	            NodeType::UnsolvedAction(_) | NodeType::LastAction => {
249	                let tag: u8 = if matches!(node.ty, NodeType::LastAction) { 1 } else { 0 };
250	                let suffix = if tag == 1 { "__lastatom" } else { "__actionatom" };
251	                let id = if port_owner_ids.contains(&node.id)
252	                    || used_dot_ids.contains(&base)
253	                {
254	                    let mut cand = format!("{base}{suffix}");
255	                    let mut n = 2u32;
256	                    while used_dot_ids.contains(&cand) {
257	                        cand = format!("{base}{suffix}{n}");
258	                        n += 1;
259	                    }
260	                    cand
261	                } else {
262	                    base
263	                };
264	                used_dot_ids.insert(id.clone());
265	                ellipse_dot_ids.insert((node.id.clone(), tag), id.clone());
266	                id
267	            }
268	        };
269	        ds_nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), id);
270	    }
271	    // 4a. Top-level (ungrouped) nodes.
272	    //
273	    // HS `dotGraphCompact` (Dot.hs:505-510) emits, in order: the FREE
274	    // (ungrouped) nodes (`mapM_ dotNodeCompact nodes`), THEN the clusters
275	    // (`mapM_ dotCluster clusters`), THEN the edges.  The free nodes — e.g. an
276	    // unsolved-action-atom ellipse like `Unlock_0(..) @ #t2.1` — therefore
277	    // appear BEFORE any `subgraph cluster_*` block.  Emit them first to match
278	    // (a free node emitted after the cluster's closing `}` lands in the wrong
279	    // scope order vs HS).
280	    for node in &repr.nodes {
281	        emit_node(&mut g, node, &abbrev_lookup, opts, &color_map, &has_outgoing,
282	            &ellipse_dot_ids);
283	    }
284	    // 4b. Clusters as subgraphs.
285	    //
286	    // HS `dotCluster` (Dot.hs:547-562): each cluster gets a `roleColor`
287	    // derived from `extractBaseName name`, the subgraph is `style=filled`
288	    // with that colour, and the colour is threaded to the child nodes as
289	    // their `manualNodeColor` (Dot.hs:562). HS also defers ALL of a
290	    // cluster's edges to `dotClustersEdges` (Dot.hs:507-510/517-522), which
291	    // runs `mergeLessEdges` over the concatenation of every cluster's edges
292	    // and emits them AFTER every node/cluster — so we collect them here.
293	    let mut cluster_edges: Vec<GEdge> = Vec::new();
294	    for (i, cluster) in repr.clusters.iter().enumerate() {
295	        // `baseName = fromMaybe "Undefined" (extractBaseName name)`.
296	        let base = extract_base_name(&cluster.name)
297	            .unwrap_or_else(|| "Undefined".to_string());
298	        let color = role_color(&base);
299	        g.open_subgraph(i, &cluster.name, &color);
300	        for node in &cluster.nodes {
301	            emit_node_colored(&mut g, node, &abbrev_lookup, opts, Some(&color),
302	                &color_map, &has_outgoing, &ellipse_dot_ids);
303	        }
304	        g.close_subgraph();
305	        cluster_edges.extend(cluster.edges.iter().cloned());
306	    }
307	    // 4c. Edges. HS emits `restEdges` (non-less) before the merged
308	    // `lessEdges` within each scope (`dotGraphCompact`, Dot.hs:508-509),
309	    // then the cluster edges last (`dotClustersEdges`).
310	    emit_edges_merged(&mut g, &repr.edges, &node_map, &ds_nodes);
311	    emit_edges_merged(&mut g, &cluster_edges, &node_map, &ds_nodes);
312	    // 4d. Legend (if any abbreviations were chosen).
313	    if !abbrevs.is_empty() {
314	        g.legend(&abbrevs);
315	    }
316	    g.close();
317	    g.into_string()
318	}
319	
320	fn emit_node(
321	    g: &mut DotBuilder,
322	    node: &GNode,
323	    abbrev: &dyn Fn(&LNTerm) -> Option<LNTerm>,
324	    opts: &GraphOptions,
325	    color_map: &NodeColorMap,
326	    has_outgoing: &HashSet<&LVar>,
327	    ellipse_dot_ids: &std::collections::BTreeMap<(LVar, u8), String>,
328	) {
329	    emit_node_colored(g, node, abbrev, opts, None, color_map, has_outgoing,
330	        ellipse_dot_ids);
331	}
332	
333	/// `emit_node` with an optional `manual_color` — the cluster `roleColor`
334	/// that HS `dotCluster` threads to its child nodes as `manualNodeColor`
335	/// (Dot.hs:562). Only the `SystemNode` branch consults it (HS
336	/// `dotNodeCompact`, Dot.hs:248-256); the other node kinds ignore it.
337	fn emit_node_colored(
338	    g: &mut DotBuilder,
339	    node: &GNode,
340	    abbrev: &dyn Fn(&LNTerm) -> Option<LNTerm>,
341	    opts: &GraphOptions,
342	    manual_color: Option<&str>,
343	    color_map: &NodeColorMap,
344	    has_outgoing: &HashSet<&LVar>,
345	    ellipse_dot_ids: &std::collections::BTreeMap<(LVar, u8), String>,
346	) {
347	    // Look up the (possibly collision-disambiguated) dot id assigned to a
348	    // non-record ellipse node (UnsolvedAction tag 0 / LastAction tag 1).
349	    let ellipse_id = |tag: u8| -> String {
350	        ellipse_dot_ids.get(&(node.id.clone(), tag)).cloned()
351	            .unwrap_or_else(|| DotBuilder::dot_node_id(&node.id))
352	    };
353	    match &node.ty {
354	        NodeType::System(ru) => {
355	            let ru_abbreviated = abbreviate_rule(ru, abbrev);
356	            let outgoing = has_outgoing.contains(&node.id);
357	            g.rule_node(&node.id, &ru_abbreviated, opts, manual_color, color_map,
358	                outgoing);
359	        }
360	        NodeType::UnsolvedAction(facts) => {
361	            let new_facts: Vec<LNFact> = facts.iter()
362	                .map(|fa| apply_abbreviations_fact(abbrev, fa))
363	                .collect();
364	            // A colliding action ellipse (same `v` as a system record) gets a
365	            // distinct dot id so both nodes survive (see `ds_nodes`).
366	            g.action_node(&ellipse_id(0), &node.id, &new_facts);
367	        }
368	        // The last-atom uses its (possibly collision-disambiguated) dot id so
369	        // it does not clash with a same-id system node.
370	        NodeType::LastAction => {
371	            g.last_node(&ellipse_id(1), &node.id);
372	        }
373	        NodeType::Missing(hint) => g.missing_node(&node.id, hint),
374	    }
375	}
376	
377	/// Emit a scope's edges in HS `dotGraphCompact` order: every non-less edge
378	/// first (`restEdges`), then the merged less-edges (`mergeLessEdges`,
379	/// Dot.hs:567-597). Because `LessAtom` equality ignores the reason, the
380	/// system holds at most one less-atom per `(smaller, larger)` pair, so the
381	/// `eqClasses` grouping is a no-op (singleton groups) — we only need to
382	/// reproduce its SORT (by `(smaller, larger)`, via `Ord LVar`) and the
383	/// single-reason colour. The gradient/`;weight` share code only fires for
384	/// multi-reason groups, which cannot arise here.
385	fn emit_edges_merged(
386	    g: &mut DotBuilder,
387	    edges: &[GEdge],
388	    node_map: &HashMap<&LVar, &RuleACInst>,
389	    ds_nodes: &std::collections::BTreeMap<LVar, String>,
390	) {
391	    // restEdges: keep original order, drop less-edges.
392	    for edge in edges {
393	        match edge {
394	            GEdge::System(src, tgt) => {
395	                g.edge(node_map, src, tgt);
396	            }
397	            GEdge::UnsolvedChain(src, tgt) => g.chain_edge(node_map, src, tgt),
398	            GEdge::Less(_) => {}
399	        }
400	    }
401	    // lessEdges: collect, sort by (smaller, larger) like `eqClasses`, emit one
402	    // merged edge per pair.
403	    let mut lesses: Vec<&LessAtom> = edges.iter()
404	        .filter_map(|e| match e { GEdge::Less(la) => Some(la), _ => None })
405	        .collect();
406	    lesses.sort_by(|a, b| (&a.smaller, &a.larger).cmp(&(&b.smaller, &b.larger)));
407	    for la in lesses {
408	        g.less_edge(la, ds_nodes);
409	    }
410	}
411	
412	fn abbreviate_rule(
413	    ru: &RuleACInst,
414	    abbrev: &dyn Fn(&LNTerm) -> Option<LNTerm>,
415	) -> RuleACInst {
416	    let mut new_ru = ru.clone();
417	    new_ru.premises = ru.premises.iter()
418	        .map(|fa| apply_abbreviations_fact(abbrev, fa))
419	        .collect();
420	    new_ru.actions = ru.actions.iter()
421	        .map(|fa| apply_abbreviations_fact(abbrev, fa))
422	        .collect();
423	    new_ru.conclusions = ru.conclusions.iter()
424	        .map(|fa| apply_abbreviations_fact(abbrev, fa))
425	        .collect();
426	    new_ru
427	}
428	
429	/// Helper used by handlers to render the [`System`] as DOT and pipe it
430	/// through `/usr/bin/dot -Tsvg` (or whatever's on `$PATH`) under the given
431	/// graph options.  Returns the SVG bytes on success.  When `dot` is
432	/// missing or fails, returns the DOT source instead (the frontend's
433	/// `intdot-staticgraph` can render DOT client-side via viz.js, so this
434	/// stays a useful response).
435	pub fn render_svg_or_dot_with(sys: &System, opts: &GraphOptions) -> RenderResult {
436	    let dot = system_to_dot_with(sys, opts);
437	    match try_render_dot_to_svg(&dot) {
438	        Ok(svg) => RenderResult::Svg(svg),
439	        Err(_) => RenderResult::Dot(dot),
440	    }
441	}
442	
443	/// What we got back from `dot`.
444	pub enum RenderResult {
445	    /// SVG bytes produced by `dot -Tsvg`.
446	    Svg(Vec<u8>),
447	    /// Raw DOT source, returned when the `dot` binary is unavailable or failed.
448	    Dot(String),
449	}
450	
451	fn try_render_dot_to_svg(dot: &str) -> std::io::Result<Vec<u8>> {
452	    use std::io::Write;
453	    use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
454	    let mut child = Command::new("dot")
455	        .args(["-Tsvg"])
456	        .stdin(Stdio::piped())
457	        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
458	        .stderr(Stdio::piped())
459	        .spawn()?;
460	    // Write the full DOT to `dot`'s stdin on a separate thread while the
461	    // main thread drains stdout/stderr via `wait_with_output`.  Doing the
462	    // (blocking) `write_all` inline before reading stdout can deadlock on
463	    // large graphs: `dot` fills its stdout pipe and blocks, and so does our
464	    // `write_all` on a full stdin pipe.
465	    let writer = child.stdin.take().map(|mut sin| {
466	        let bytes = dot.as_bytes().to_vec();
467	        std::thread::spawn(move || sin.write_all(&bytes))
468	    });
469	    let out = child.wait_with_output()?;
470	    if let Some(handle) = writer {
471	        // Propagate any write error (ignore a panicked thread).
472	        if let Ok(res) = handle.join() {
473	            res?;
474	        }
475	    }
476	    if !out.status.success() {
477	        return Err(std::io::Error::other(
478	            format!("dot exited with status {:?}", out.status)));
479	    }
480	    Ok(out.stdout)
481	}
482	
483	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
484	// DOT construction
485	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
486	
487	struct DotBuilder {
488	    buf: String,
489	}
490	
491	impl DotBuilder {
492	    fn new() -> Self {
493	        DotBuilder { buf: String::new() }
494	    }
495	    fn into_string(self) -> String { self.buf }
496	    fn preamble(&mut self, has_clusters: bool) {
497	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "digraph G {{");
498	        if has_clusters {
499	            // HS `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` (Dot.hs:140-161): a richer
500	            // attribute block for clustered graphs.
501	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  nodesep=0.8; ranksep=0.8;");
502	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  sep=4;");
503	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  splines=true;");
504	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  overlap=false;");
505	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  pack=true;");
506	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  packmode=cluster;");
507	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  concentrate=true;");
508	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  compound=true;");
509	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  remincross=true;");
510	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  mclimit=10;");
511	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  nslimit=20;");
512	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  nslimit1=20;");
513	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  ordering=out;");
514	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  rankdir=TB;");
515	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  showboxes=false;");
516	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  clusterrank=local;");
517	            // HS `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` sets the graph-level node
518	            // default shape to `ellipse` (Dot.hs:160); each compact rule node
519	            // overrides it with its own per-node `shape=record` (emitted in
520	            // `rule_node`, mirroring HS `genRecord "record"`), so record rules
521	            // still render as records inside clusters.
522	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
523	                "  node [fontsize=8,fontname=\"Helvetica\",width=0.3,height=0.2,margin=\"0.05,0.05\",shape=ellipse];");
524	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
525	                "  edge [fontsize=8,fontname=\"Helvetica\",penwidth=1.5,arrowsize=0.5,color=black,style=solid,weight=8];");
526	        } else {
527	            // HS `setDefaultAttributes` (Dot.hs:130-135). Note the node
528	            // `width=0.3,height=0.2` defaults HS emits; we additionally keep
529	            // `shape=record` because each rule node is rendered as a record
530	            // label (HS sets the record shape per-node via `D.record`).
531	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  nodesep=0.3; ranksep=0.3;");
532	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
533	                "  node [fontsize=8,fontname=\"Helvetica\",width=0.3,height=0.2,shape=record];");
534	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
535	                "  edge [fontsize=8,fontname=\"Helvetica\"];");
536	        }
537	    }
538	    fn close(&mut self) {
539	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "}}");
540	    }
541	    fn dot_node_id(nid: &LVar) -> String {
542	        // Sanitise to a valid DOT identifier.
543	        let raw = format!("{}_{}", nid.name, nid.idx);
544	        raw.chars().map(|c| if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() { c } else { '_' })
545	            .collect()
546	    }
547	    fn rule_node(&mut self, nid: &LVar, ru: &RuleACInst, opts: &GraphOptions,
548	                 manual_color: Option<&str>, color_map: &NodeColorMap,
549	                 outgoing: bool) {
550	        let id = Self::dot_node_id(nid);
551	        // HS `mkNode`'s `CompactBoringNodes` branch (Dot.hs:294-304): under the
552	        // default node style (`defaultDotOptions = DotOptions CompactBoringNodes`,
553	        // Dot.hs:82; the interactive route builds its `DotOptions` from
554	        // `getOptions`, Handler.hs:1334/1348, defaulting to `CompactBoringNodes`
555	        // when the `uncompact` query param is absent), an intruder rule or the
556	        // `Fresh` rule collapses to a plain `mkSimpleNode` ellipse (Dot.hs:289-290)
557	        // with NO fill/font/role attrs. Its label is `show v : showDotRuleCaseName
558	        // ru` when the node has an outgoing edge, else the full rule label incl.
559	        // the bracketed action row (`concatMap snd as` = `ruleLabelM`,
560	        // Dot.hs:301-302/330-338). The ellipse label is a PLAIN string, so it is
561	        // escaped with `escape_dot_label` (HS `showAttr`, Dot.hs:346-353), NOT the
562	        // record-field `escape_dot`.
563	        if is_intruder_or_fresh(ru) {
564	            let lbl = if outgoing {
565	                format!("{} : {}", nid, rule_case_name(ru))
566	            } else {
567	                // HS `concatMap snd as` (Dot.hs:302) — `as` is the
568	                // `renderRow`-rendered rule label, i.e. the SAME
569	                // `renderBalanced` single-doc row (width 130) the record
570	                // mid row uses.  No `fixMultiLineLabel` here: this label
571	                // goes through plain `mkSimpleNode` → `D.node` → `showAttr`
572	                // (spaces stay spaces; `\n` → `\l` via `escape_dot_label`).
573	                render_balanced(vec![rule_label_doc(nid, ru, opts)])
574	                    .pop()
575	                    .unwrap_or_default()
576	            };
577	            let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
578	                "  {} [label=\"{}\",shape=ellipse];",
579	                id, escape_dot_label(&lbl));
580	            return;
581	        }
582	        // Build prems / acts / concs rows as Docs, then lay each row out with
583	        // HS `renderRow`/`renderBalanced` (Dot.hs:357-379): every field of a
584	        // row is rendered at a width proportional to its one-line length
585	        // (total 100, `max 30 . round . (*1.3)`), NOT at the page width.
586	        let prem_docs: Vec<Doc> = ru.premises.iter().map(fact_doc_of).collect();
587	        let conc_docs: Vec<Doc> = ru.conclusions.iter().map(fact_doc_of).collect();
588	        let ps = render_balanced(prem_docs);
589	        let mid = render_balanced(vec![rule_label_doc(nid, ru, opts)])
590	            .pop()
591	            .unwrap_or_default();
592	        let cs = render_balanced(conc_docs);
593	        // Record label — HS `D.vcat $ map D.hcat $ … $ filter (not . null)
594	        // [ps, as, cs]` (Dot.hs:310-312) rendered by `Text.Dot.renderRecord`
595	        // (Text/Dot.hs:254-280): the outer VCat renders as `{row|row|row}`,
596	        // each row (HCat) as `{field|field}`, each ported field as
597	        // `<port> text`.  Field text goes through `fixMultiLineLabel`
598	        // (mkField, Text/Dot.hs:378-381: leading spaces → ` `, plus a
599	        // trailing newline via `unlines` when multi-line) and the
600	        // record-metachar escape (`| { } < >`, Text/Dot.hs:273-280).  The
601	        // remaining newlines become `\l` at the attribute level
602	        // (`showAttr`, Text/Dot.hs:346-353) via `escape_dot_label`.
603	        let mut rows: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
604	        if !ps.is_empty() {
605	            rows.push(format!("{{{}}}", ps.iter().enumerate()
606	                .map(|(i, s)| format!(
607	                    "<p{}> {}", i, escape_record_field(&fix_multi_line_label(s))))
608	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
609	                .join("|")));
610	        }
611	        rows.push(format!("{{{}}}",
612	            escape_record_field(&fix_multi_line_label(&mid))));
613	        if !cs.is_empty() {
614	            rows.push(format!("{{{}}}", cs.iter().enumerate()
615	                .map(|(i, s)| format!(
616	                    "<c{}> {}", i, escape_record_field(&fix_multi_line_label(s))))
617	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
618	                .join("|")));
619	        }
620	        let lbl = escape_dot_label(&format!("{{{}}}", rows.join("|")));
621	        let color = rule_fillcolor(ru, manual_color, color_map);
622	        // HS `dotNodeCompact` record `attrs` (Dot.hs:257-259) also carry a
623	        // `fontcolor` and a `role`. The `fontcolor` keys off the PALETTE colour
624	        // (`M.lookup rInfoVal colorMap`), i.e. the raw map value — NOT the
625	        // resolved `fillcolor` — so an explicit/cluster override does not change
626	        // the font choice. `role = fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)`
627	        // (Dot.hs:243).
628	        let palette_color = color_map.lookup(&ru.info);
629	        let fontcolor = if color_uses_white_font(palette_color) {
630	            "white"
631	        } else {
632	            "black"
633	        };
634	        let role = extract_role(ru).unwrap_or("Undefined");
635	        // HS `genRecord "record"` (Text/Dot.hs:284-288) prepends an explicit
636	        // `("shape","record")` to every compact record node, then the label and
637	        // the `dotNodeCompact` `attrs`.  The per-node `shape=record` OVERRIDES the
638	        // graph-level default node shape — which is `record` in the flat
639	        // `setDefaultAttributes` case but `ellipse` in the clustered
640	        // `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` case (Dot.hs:160).  Emit it explicitly
641	        // so clustered SAPIC graphs keep `shape=record` (not the ellipse default).
642	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
643	            "  {} [shape=record,label=\"{}\",style=\"filled\",fillcolor=\"{}\",fontcolor=\"{}\",role=\"{}\"];",
644	            id, lbl, color, fontcolor, escape_dot_label(role));
645	    }
646	    fn action_node(&mut self, id: &str, nid: &LVar, facts: &[LNFact]) {
647	        // HS `lblPre <- fsep <$> punctuate comma <$> mapM renderLNFact facts;
648	        // lbl = lblPre <-> opAction <-> text (show v); mkSimpleNode (render
649	        // lbl) attrs` (Dot.hs:267-272): the WHOLE label is ONE Doc — the
650	        // facts fill-wrap as a paragraph — rendered by the default-style
651	        // `render` (HughesPJ `style`: lineLength 100, ribbon 67), NOT one
652	        // fact at a time.  `opAction = operator_ "@"`, `<->` is space-joined,
653	        // and `show v` renders via `Display for LVar` (e.g. `#i` / `#i.2`).
654	        let fact_docs: Vec<Doc> = facts.iter().map(fact_doc_of).collect();
655	        let s = pretty_hpj::fsep(pretty_hpj::punctuate(Doc::text(","), fact_docs))
656	            .beside_sp(Doc::text("@"))
657	            .beside_sp(Doc::text(nid.to_string()))
658	            .render_with(WEB_LINE_LENGTH, WEB_RIBBON);
659	        let color = if facts.iter().any(|f| matches!(f.tag, FactTag::Ku)) {
660	            "gray"
661	        } else { "darkblue" };
662	        // HS renders a loose action node via `mkSimpleNode (render lbl) attrs`
663	        // = plain `D.node [("label", …), ("shape","ellipse")]` (Dot.hs:267-272,
664	        // 289-290), NOT `D.record`.  A plain node label is a quoted string whose
665	        // only metacharacters are `"` and newline (`escape_dot_label` =
666	        // `showAttr`, Text/Dot.hs:346-353); the record metacharacters
667	        // `{ } | < >` are LITERAL, so a tuple `<A, B, …>` in a goal fact must
668	        // stay `<…>` and NOT be `\<…\>`-escaped (only the `SystemNode`/
669	        // `D.record` path escapes them).
670	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
671	            "  {} [shape=ellipse,label=\"{}\",color=\"{}\"];",
672	            id, escape_dot_label(&s), color);
673	    }
674	    fn last_node(&mut self, dot_id: &str, nid: &LVar) {
675	        // HS `LastActionAtom -> mkSimpleNode (show v) []` (Dot.hs:273): the
676	        // label is `show v`, rendered via `Display for LVar` (`#i` / `#i.2`),
677	        // via plain `D.node` (see `action_node`), so use the plain-label escaper.
678	        // `dot_id` is the collision-disambiguated id (see `ellipse_dot_ids`).
679	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
680	            "  {} [shape=ellipse,label=\"{}\"];",
681	            dot_id, escape_dot_label(&nid.to_string()));
682	    }
683	    fn missing_node(&mut self, nid: &LVar, hint: &MissingHint) {
684	        let id = Self::dot_node_id(nid);
685	        // Mirror Haskell `dotNodeCompact` (Dot.hs:274-282): a
686	        // missing-conclusion node is a `trapezium` labelled `prettyNodeConc`,
687	        // a missing-premise node is an `invtrapezium` labelled `prettyNodePrem`.
688	        // Both labels are `parens (prettyNodeId v <> comma <-> int i)`
689	        // (Constraints.hs:251/255), i.e. `(<show v>, <i>)` — the conclusion /
690	        // premise index is part of the label, not dropped.
691	        let (shape, idx) = match hint {
692	            MissingHint::Conc(ci) => ("trapezium", ci.0),
693	            MissingHint::Prem(pi) => ("invtrapezium", pi.0),
694	        };
695	        let label = format!("({}, {})", nid, idx);
696	        // HS `dotConcC`/`dotPremC` = `missingNode shape (render label)` = plain
697	        // `D.node` (Dot.hs:280-282), so use the plain-label escaper (matching
698	        // `action_node`/`last_node`).  This label (`(#i, 0)`) never contains
699	        // record metacharacters, so the choice is inert here, but keeping all
700	        // three plain (ellipse/trapezium) nodes on `escape_dot_label` mirrors HS.
701	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
702	            "  {} [shape={},label=\"{}\"];",
703	            id, shape, escape_dot_label(&label));
704	    }
705	    fn edge(&mut self,
706	            node_map: &HashMap<&LVar, &RuleACInst>,
707	            src: &tamarin_theory::constraint::constraints::NodeConc,
708	            tgt: &tamarin_theory::constraint::constraints::NodePrem) {
709	        // Look up the target premise's fact tag so we can colour
710	        // the edge.
711	        let style = edge_style(node_map, src, tgt);
712	        let src_ref = conc_port_ref(node_map, src);
713	        let tgt_ref = prem_port_ref(node_map, tgt);
714	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
715	            "  {} -> {} [{}];", src_ref, tgt_ref, style);
716	    }
717	    fn chain_edge(&mut self,
718	                  node_map: &HashMap<&LVar, &RuleACInst>,
719	                  src: &tamarin_theory::constraint::constraints::NodeConc,
720	                  tgt: &tamarin_theory::constraint::constraints::NodePrem) {
721	        let src_ref = conc_port_ref(node_map, src);
722	        let tgt_ref = prem_port_ref(node_map, tgt);
723	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
724	            "  {} -> {} [style=\"dotted\",color=\"green\"];",
725	            src_ref, tgt_ref);
726	    }
727	    /// Open a subgraph (Graphviz `subgraph cluster_<n> { ... }`).
728	    /// `idx` is a numeric disambiguator; `name` is shown as the label and
729	    /// `color` is the cluster's `roleColor` (HS `dotCluster`, Dot.hs:547-562).
730	    ///
731	    /// The attribute block mirrors HS `dotCluster`'s sequence exactly:
732	    /// `nodesep=0.6`, `ranksep=0.6`, `label`, `style=filled`, `color`,
733	    /// `penwidth=2`, `fillcolor`, `overlap=false`, `sep=4`. (The subgraph id
734	    /// `cluster_<n>` is the Rust convention — HS uses
735	    /// `createClusterNodeId roleName` — but the styling attributes are
736	    /// byte-faithful.)
737	    fn open_subgraph(&mut self, idx: usize, name: &str, color: &str) {
738	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  subgraph cluster_{} {{", idx);
739	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    nodesep=\"0.6\";");
740	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    ranksep=\"0.6\";");
741	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    label=\"{}\";", escape_dot_label(name));
742	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    style=\"filled\";");
743	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    color=\"{}\";", color);
744	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    penwidth=\"2\";");
745	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    fillcolor=\"{}\";", color);
746	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    overlap=\"false\";");
747	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "    sep=\"4\";");
748	    }
749	    fn close_subgraph(&mut self) {
750	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  }}");
751	    }
752	    /// Emit a single merged less-edge. HS `dotLessEdge` (Dot.hs:406-410)
753	    /// emits the attributes `[("color",color),("style","dashed")]` — colour
754	    /// FIRST, then style. The colour is `allRtoColors` of the group's
755	    /// reasons; since at most one less-atom survives per node pair (LessAtom
756	    /// equality ignores the reason), the group is a singleton and the colour
757	    /// reduces to the single reason's `toColor` (`reason_color`).
758	    fn less_edge(&mut self, la: &LessAtom,
759	                 ds_nodes: &std::collections::BTreeMap<LVar, String>) {
760	        // HS `dotLessEdge` resolves each endpoint through `dsNodes` (Dot.hs:408-409),
761	        // which holds the LAST dot node emitted at that id — the action / last
762	        // ellipse when it shadows a same-id system record. Mirror that by
763	        // resolving through the precomputed `ds_nodes` map (falling back to the
764	        // bare id for any endpoint that was never emitted as a node).
765	        let resolve = |nid: &LVar| -> String {
766	            ds_nodes.get(nid).cloned()
767	                .unwrap_or_else(|| Self::dot_node_id(nid))
768	        };
769	        let s = resolve(&la.smaller);
770	        let t = resolve(&la.larger);
771	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf,
772	            "  {} -> {} [color=\"{}\",style=\"dashed\"];",
773	            s, t, reason_color(la.reason));
774	    }
775	    /// Emit a legend node listing the chosen abbreviations.
776	    /// Mirror of Haskell's `generateLegend` (Dot.hs:415-474) — produces a
777	    /// single DOT node with an HTML-table label of `name = expansion` rows.
778	    /// Rows are ordered by `topoSortAbbrevs` applied to a descending sort
779	    /// of the rendered abbreviation names, so that an abbreviation used
780	    /// inside another's expansion is printed first.
781	    fn legend(&mut self, abbrevs: &Abbreviations) {
782	        // sortOn (Down . render . prettyLNTerm . fst) $ M.elems abbrevs
783	        // M.elems iterates by key (orig term) order; sortOn is stable.
784	        let mut entries: Vec<(&LNTerm, &LNTerm)> = abbrevs.iter()
785	            .map(|(_orig, (name, exp))| (name, exp))
786	            .collect();
787	        // Descending by rendered name (stable); key cached per element.
788	        entries.sort_by_key(|x| std::cmp::Reverse(pretty_lnterm(x.0)));
789	        let order = topo_sort_abbrevs(&entries);
790	        // Mirror Haskell `abbrevLabel`: tableAttributes =
791	        //   [Border 1, CellBorder 0, CellSpacing 3, CellPadding 1].
792	        let mut html = String::new();
793	        html.push_str(
794	            "<<TABLE BORDER=\"1\" CELLBORDER=\"0\" CELLSPACING=\"3\" CELLPADDING=\"1\">");
795	        // Mirror Haskell `renderLine` (Dot.hs:441-450): each row is three
796	        // `LabelCell`s with `cellAttributes = [Align HLeft, VAlign HTop]`.
797	        // The NAME cell wraps its text in `<FONT COLOR="labelColor">`
798	        // (`font txt = Text [Font [Color labelColor] txt]`), while the `=`
799	        // and expansion cells are bare `Text`.  `labelColor = doAbbrevColor`
800	        // (`defaultDotOptions = DotOptions CompactBoringNodes black`,
801	        // Dot.hs:82; the web route never overrides `_doAbbrevColor`), which
802	        // renders as `#000000`.  The graphviz HTML-table printer emits the
803	        // cells of a `Cells` row separated by a single space and each `<TR>`
804	        // on its own line, so we join the three cells with `" "` and the rows
805	        // with `"\n"`.
806	        let rows: Vec<String> = order.iter().map(|&i| {
807	            let (name, exp) = entries[i];
808	            let name_cell = format!(
809	                "<TD ALIGN=\"LEFT\" VALIGN=\"TOP\"><FONT COLOR=\"#000000\">{}</FONT></TD>",
810	                dot_html_escape(&pretty_lnterm(name)));
811	            let eq_cell = "<TD ALIGN=\"LEFT\" VALIGN=\"TOP\">=</TD>".to_string();
812	            let exp_cell = format!(
813	                "<TD ALIGN=\"LEFT\" VALIGN=\"TOP\">{}</TD>",
814	                dot_html_escape(&pretty_lnterm(exp)));
815	            format!("<TR>{}</TR>", [name_cell, eq_cell, exp_cell].join(" "))
816	        }).collect();
817	        html.push_str(&rows.join("\n"));
818	        html.push_str("</TABLE>>");
819	        // HS `generateLegend` (Dot.hs:419-425) emits the legend inside a
820	        // `D.scope` carrying `rank="sink"` — i.e. `{ rank="sink"; <node>; }` —
821	        // then adds invisible sink→legend edges purely for layout (which we
822	        // omit, matching the parity comparator that drops `style=invis`
823	        // edges).  Reproduce the scope: besides mirroring HS's structure, the
824	        // leading `rank="sink";` statement keeps the legend a self-contained
825	        // statement.  A bare top-level `legend [...]` node emitted right after
826	        // a cluster's brace-terminated (`}`, no `;`) close would otherwise be
827	        // glued to that `}` by a naive statement splitter and lost.  Haskell
828	        // emits shape "plain".
829	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  {{");
830	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  rank=\"sink\";");
831	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  legend [shape=plain,label={}];", html);
832	        let _ = writeln!(self.buf, "  }}");
833	    }
834	}
835	
836	/// Mirror Haskell `topoSortAbbrevs` (Dot.hs:459-474).
837	///
838	/// `entries` is the descending-name-sorted list of `(name, expansion)`.
839	/// We build a graph with an edge `v -> u` whenever `entries[v].name` is a
840	/// proper subterm of `entries[u].expansion` (i.e. abbreviation `v` is used
841	/// inside abbreviation `u`), then return vertices in topological order so
842	/// that used-inside abbreviations are printed first.
843	///
844	/// This reproduces `Data.Graph.graphFromEdges` + `Data.Graph.topSort`:
845	/// keys are `[0..]` in the given order (already sorted), so vertex `i`
846	/// corresponds to `entries[i]`; `topSort = reverse . postorder` of the DFS
847	/// forest taken over vertices `0..n-1` in order.
848	fn topo_sort_abbrevs(entries: &[(&LNTerm, &LNTerm)]) -> Vec<usize> {
849	    use tamarin_term::term::is_proper_subterm;
850	    let n = entries.len();
851	    // Adjacency: successors of v in ascending vertex order (findLegendEdges
852	    // iterates keyedElems in order, so target keys/vertices are ascending).
853	    let adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = (0..n)
854	        .map(|v| {
855	            (0..n)
856	                .filter(|&u| is_proper_subterm(entries[v].0, entries[u].1))
857	                .collect()
858	        })
859	        .collect();
860	    // DFS forest over vertices 0..n-1, collecting postorder.
861	    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
862	    let mut postorder: Vec<usize> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
863	    // Iterative DFS that emits a vertex on exit (postorder).
864	    for start in 0..n {
865	        if visited[start] {
866	            continue;
867	        }
868	        // Stack of (vertex, next-successor-index).
869	        let mut stack: Vec<(usize, usize)> = Vec::new();
870	        visited[start] = true;
871	        stack.push((start, 0));
872	        while let Some(&(v, idx)) = stack.last() {
873	            if idx < adj[v].len() {
874	                let w = adj[v][idx];
875	                stack.last_mut().unwrap().1 += 1;
876	                if !visited[w] {
877	                    visited[w] = true;
878	                    stack.push((w, 0));
879	                }
880	            } else {
881	                postorder.push(v);
882	                stack.pop();
883	            }
884	        }
885	    }
886	    // topSort = reverse postorder.
887	    postorder.reverse();
888	    postorder
889	}
890	
891	/// HTML-escape a string for use in a Graphviz HTML-like label.
892	/// Distinct from `crate::handlers::root::html_escape` (which also escapes
893	/// `'`) because it targets a different context (DOT HTML-like label vs a
894	/// general HTML page); do NOT merge the two char sets.
895	fn dot_html_escape(s: &str) -> String {
896	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len());
897	    for c in s.chars() {
898	        match c {
899	            '<' => out.push_str("<"),
900	            '>' => out.push_str(">"),
901	            '&' => out.push_str("&"),
902	            '"' => out.push_str("""),
903	            _ => out.push(c),
904	        }
905	    }
906	    out
907	}
908	
909	/// The `Doc` of an `LNFact` exactly as Haskell `renderLNFact =
910	/// prettyLNFact` (Dot.hs:225-233, Fact.hs:551).  `prettyLNFact` builds the
911	/// argument list with `nestShort' (n++"(") ")" . fsep . punctuate comma`
912	/// (Fact.hs:539-546), which — unlike a bare `name(a, b)` — emits the
913	/// HughesPJ INNER-PAREN SPACES `!KU( ~ltk )` when the fact fits on one line.
914	/// We therefore reuse the *same* faithful `Doc` path the proof pretty-
915	/// printer uses for goals (`solve_goal_to_doc` → `pretty_formula::fact_doc`
916	/// on the parser-AST projection), NOT `pretty_system::pretty_fact` (which
917	/// omits those spaces).
918	fn fact_doc_of(fa: &LNFact) -> Doc {
919	    tamarin_theory::pretty_formula::fact_doc(
920	        &tamarin_theory::pretty_theory::lnfact_to_parser(fa),
921	    )
922	}
923	
924	/// Haskell `round :: Double -> Int` — IEEE round-half-to-EVEN (banker's
925	/// rounding), unlike Rust's `f64::round` (half-away-from-zero).  The
926	/// balanced widths (`conv = max 30 . round . (*1.3)`), the ribbon
927	/// (`round (w / 1.5)` in `fullRender`) and `scaleIndent`'s space count all
928	/// go through HS `round`, and half cases DO occur (e.g. `130/1.5 =
929	/// 86.66→87`, `1.5*23 = 34.5→34`).
930	fn round_half_even(x: f64) -> i64 {
931	    let f = x.floor();
932	    let diff = x - f;
933	    if diff > 0.5 {
934	        f as i64 + 1
935	    } else if diff < 0.5 {
936	        f as i64
937	    } else {
938	        let fi = f as i64;
939	        if fi % 2 == 0 { fi } else { fi + 1 }
940	    }
941	}
942	
943	/// HS `renderBalanced 100 (max 30 . round . (*1.3))` + `scaleIndent`
944	/// (Dot.hs:357-379), the layout engine for record-row fields: each doc of
945	/// a row is rendered at a line length PROPORTIONAL to its one-line length
946	/// (`renderStyle (defaultStyle { lineLength = w })`, i.e. PageMode with
947	/// ribbon `round (w / 1.5)`), so a lone fact in a row gets width
948	/// `max 30 (round 130) = 130` (ribbon 87) while four facts share the
949	/// 100-column budget.  `usedWidths` measure the OneLineMode render
950	/// (`Doc::one_line_render`), which turns every fill/sep break point into
951	/// one space.
952	///
953	/// `scaleIndent` is applied to the WHOLE rendered string (HS's `line`
954	/// binding spans the full render): `span isSpace` therefore only rescales
955	/// whitespace at the very START of the FIRST line — a no-op for every
956	/// label whose first char is text, exactly as in HS.
957	fn render_balanced(docs: Vec<Doc>) -> Vec<String> {
958	    if docs.is_empty() {
959	        return Vec::new();
960	    }
961	    let used: Vec<f64> = docs.iter()
962	        .map(|d| d.one_line_render().chars().count() as f64)
963	        .collect();
964	    let total: f64 = used.iter().sum();
965	    let ratio = 100.0 / total;
966	    docs.into_iter()
967	        .zip(used)
968	        .map(|(d, u)| {
969	            // conv (ratio * w) with conv = max 30 . round . (*1.3).
970	            let w = std::cmp::max(30, round_half_even((ratio * u) * 1.3));
971	            // `renderStyle (defaultStyle { lineLength = w })` keeps
972	            // ribbonsPerLine = 1.5 → ribbon = round (w / 1.5)
973	            // (pretty-1.1.3.6 `fullRender`).
974	            let ribbon = round_half_even(w as f64 / 1.5);
975	            scale_indent(d.render_with(w as usize, ribbon as usize))
976	        })
977	        .collect()
978	}
979	
980	/// HS `scaleIndent` (Dot.hs:375-379) — see `render_balanced`.
981	fn scale_indent(s: String) -> String {
982	    let leading = s.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count();
983	    if leading == 0 {
984	        return s;
985	    }
986	    let rest: String = s.chars().skip(leading).collect();

content.text: Decisive result: GOT and EXP are both exactly 1766 bytes and byte-identical **up to byte 426** — the entire preamble, record structure, the `Ack` cell wrap, all 10 KU ellipses, the compressed/invtrapezium nodes, every edge, and all `nK` ids/ports match HS exactly. The **only** divergence is the wrap position *inside the `Big` cell*: HS packs `x8.4` onto the first line, the clean engine breaks one element earlier. Let me compute the exact width math to pin the root cause.

input.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
def rhe(x):  # round-half-to-even
    import math
    f=math.floor(x); d=x-f
    if d>0.5: return f+1
    if d<0.5: return f
    return f if f%2==0 else f+1

cells = {
 "Ack": "Ack( ~n.4, <x1.4, x2.4> )",
 "Big": "Big( <x1.4, x2.4, x3.4, x4.4, x5.4, x6.4, x7.4, x8.4, x9.4, x10.4> )",
 "Out": "Out( x1.4 )",
}
flats = {k: len(v) for k,v in cells.items()}
print("flat widths:", flats)
total = sum(flats.values())
print("group total:", total, " (>87 =>", total>87, ")")
print()
print(f"{'cell':4} {'flat':>4} {'clean_budget':>12} {'HS_field_w':>10} {'HS_ribbon':>9}")
for k in cells:
    others = total - flats[k]
    clean_budget = max(87 - others, 20)
    w = max(30, rhe(1.3*(100*flats[k]/total)))
    ribbon = rhe(w/1.5)
    print(f"{k:4} {flats[k]:>4} {clean_budget:>12} {w:>10} {ribbon:>9}")
PY

content.text: My flat-count differs slightly (Out=11 vs B6's 14) but the mechanism is unambiguous. For the **Big** cell: clean's group-budget = **51**, but HS's proportional field ribbon = **57**. HS fills to ~57 columns (fits `x8.4`), clean breaks at 51 (before `x8.4`). This is the proportional-per-field-width-vs-flat-group-budget difference the round-5 report identified — B6's `cell_budget` approximation matches `Ack`/`Out` but not `Big`. Let me confirm whether feeding the clean fill the HS ribbon width (57) reproduces HS's `Big` bytes, to pinpoint whether it's purely the budget formula.

content.text: Precise root cause pinned: the clean fill/peel/escape algorithm is byte-faithful (it reproduces HS's `Big` cell exactly at budget 55–57), but B6's `cell_budget` formula `max(87−Σothers,20)` yields **51** for `Big`, where HS's proportional field ribbon is **57** (`round(max(30, round(1.3·100·68/104))/1.5)` = `round(85/1.5)` = 57). B6's flat group-budget matches `Ack`/`Out` but breaks `Big` one element early. Fixing it requires implementing HS's proportional `renderBalanced` per-field width inside the clean crate — a clean-side logic change I'm forbidden to make. So B's byte-exact swap is blocked. # Dirty-room integration report — round-5 closures, units E (macros) + B (graph)

Date: 2026-07-17. Integrator: dirty-room (adapters + extraction only; no logic
transplanted from replaced files into clean code). Repo: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs`.
Same protocol/precedent. Rebased on the CURRENT tree (the unit-G SWAP and the
round-6 unit-D re-sync already applied; header count inherited at 133). Similarity
audits PASSED for A, B, E; only B and E in scope this pass. Outcome: **both
vendored clean trees RE-SYNCED (macros round-5 = staged mode; graph round-6 =
group-budget wrap trigger); BOTH headline swaps stay KEPT with precise,
live/test-confirmed blockers.** No headered file deleted → header count unchanged
(133 → 133).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## E. Macros (macro-clean) round-5 — RE-SYNCED (staged mode); rewire BLOCKED

Re-synced `crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs` <- macros round-5 workspace
`lib.rs` (mechanical: drop `pub mod ast;`, `use ast::*;`->`use
tamarin_parser::ast::*;`, drop the external `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;`). It now
adds the consumer STAGED mode the round-4 report asked for: a private `Mode`
{`FullClose`,`Staged`}; `pub fn expand` (= FullClose, unchanged) and a new `pub
fn expand_staged` that (a) leaves `AccLemma`/`CaseTest` formulas byte-identical
(`it.clone()`) and (b) rewrites ONLY the primary rule form (`variants` /
`left_right` carried through unchanged). Compiles; all 512 lines equal the
workspace file after the three transforms; stays headerless (verified).

REWIRE (route `macro_expand::expand_theory_macros` / `macro_expanded_clone`
through `macros::expand_staged`) PROBED then REVERTED. The staged mode fixes the
two round-4 over-expansion defects (AccLemma/CaseTest untouched; variants
passthrough — the captured `acc_lemma_formula_is_not_macro_expanded` /
`case_test_formula_is_not_macro_expanded` both PASS through the clean staged
entry). **But a THIRD captured test — `bare_nullary_macro_name_expands` (a hard
gate, must pass UNMODIFIED) — FAILS against the clean expander** at
`macro_expand.rs:373`:

    got App("h", [PubLit("seed")])     // expected the var `konst.1` untouched

The theory has `macros: konst() = h('seed')` and an action `M(konst.1,
konst:pub)`. HS's `nullaryApp` parser treats a bare arity-0 macro name as a
nullary CALL only when it is fully undecorated; a name carrying an index (`.1`)
or a sort suffix (`:pub`) backtracks to an ordinary variable. The ported
`apply_macros_term` reproduces this exactly (`v.idx == 0 && v.sort == Untagged &&
v.typ.is_none()`). The clean `expand_term` Var arm checks only `v.sort ==
Untagged && formals.is_empty()` — it **ignores `idx` and `typ`** — so it
over-expands `konst.1` to the macro body (`konst:pub` is fine: it parses to
`Suffix(Pub)`, ≠ `Untagged`). The clean crate's own test corpus never builds an
indexed `VarSpec` (`idx: 0` everywhere), so its oracle never observed this case.
Closing it requires adding the `idx == 0 && typ.is_none()` discrimination to the
clean `expand_term` — a behavioral logic change to a clean (headerless) file,
which the protocol forbids the dirty room (clean files get mechanical fixes
only; this is unobserved-case behavior the clean crate must derive on its own
side). No adapter can bridge it (it can't tell a spuriously-expanded body from a
legitimate one after the fact). Per "never force a swap": KEPT ported
`macro_expand.rs` (`expand_theory_macros`/`expand_items`/`expand_rule`, header
intact — verified byte-identical to HEAD except the pre-existing GitHub-username
header migration). Deleted: none. Header: 0. A future close needs a
`idx==0 && typ.is_none()` bare-nullary guard in the clean expander (clean-side).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## B. Graph serialization swap (system_to_dot -> clean generate) — NOT PERFORMED

Re-synced `crates/tamarin-server/src/graph_clean/` <- graph round-6 workspace:
only `generate.rs` (+23 lines) and `render.rs` (+205 lines) changed vs the
round-5 vendored copy; the other six files are byte-stable after `crate::`->
`super::`. Round-6 closes the round-5 wrap TRIGGER: `render.rs` adds
`MIN_CELL_BUDGET=20`, `cell_budget(flats,i) = max(87 − Σ others, 20)`,
`wrap_cell_budget(flat,budget)` and `count_info_actions`; `generate::group_cells`
shares that budget across a premise/conclusion group (a cell wraps iff the group
total exceeds the fill width). All 23 graph_clean inline tests pass, incl. the
new `group_trigger_matches_wide_record` and `multi_arg_fact_break_drops_the_comma_
space` (the `Ack( ~n.4, <x1.4, x2.4> )` case). Headerless (verified).

**Live byte gate REBUILT and RUN** (graphdot reference-server recipe, PATH
`/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin`, port 3211; HS invoked with `--port=3211`
[equals-form — the oracle script's space-form `--port 3211` makes cmdargs read
the port as WORKDIR]). A purpose-built `Wide` probe theory (10-tuple `In`, three
wide conclusions `[Ack, Big, Out]`) captured fresh at `interactive-graph-def/
cases/raw/1/1`. To exercise the ACTUAL swap path, a scratch harness built the
clean `System` from the FLAT cell strings (the `RawRule` seam: pre-rendered
premise/info/conclusion text) plus the compressed `isend` ellipse, 10 `!KU`
knowledge ellipses, the `(#i, 0)` invtrapezium, and the structural / message /
knowledge-deduction edges, then `to_dot(generate(&sys))`.

Result: **1766 bytes vs 1766 bytes, byte-identical through byte 425 — then
DIVERGES inside the `Big` conclusion cell.** Everything else matches HS exactly:
the `digraph "G"` header, the whole record structure, the `Ack` cell wrap
(`Ack( ~n.4,\l     \<x1.4, x2.4\>\l)\l` — the round-5
blocker, now REPRODUCED), all `<nK>` ports and node ids (ports n0-n5 then node
n6, then n7…n18 — the clean allocator matches HS positionally), every ellipse,
and every edge. The single divergence:

    clean:  … x6.4, x7.4, \l … x8.4, x9.4, x10.4 …   (breaks before x8.4)
    HS:     … x6.4, x7.4, x8.4, \l … x9.4, x10.4 …   (breaks after  x8.4)

Root cause (pinned): the group-budget FORMULA. For the conclusion group
`[Ack 25, Big 68, Out 11]` (Σ 104): clean `cell_budget(Big) = max(87 − 36, 20) =
51`; HS renders `Big` at its PROPORTIONAL field width `renderBalanced`
`max(30, round(1.3·100·68/104)) = 85`, ribbon `round(85/1.5) = 57` (`handlers/
dot.rs::render_balanced`). Feeding the clean `wrap_cell_budget(Big, b)` any `b`
in {55,56,57} reproduces HS's `Big` cell byte-for-byte, and `b=51` breaks one
element early — so the clean FILL/peel/escape engine is byte-faithful; only the
per-cell BUDGET is wrong. B6's flat `max(87 − Σ others, 20)` coincides with HS's
proportional ribbon at the floor (`Ack`: clean 20 vs HS ribbon 21 — same break)
and where no wrap occurs (`Out`), but not for a wide cell sharing a group
(`Big`: 51 vs 57). This is exactly the round-5 diagnosis — HS measures a field
against its shrunken PROPORTIONAL share (`max 30 . round . (*1.3)` over a 100-col
budget + per-field ribbon `round(w/1.5)`), not a flat 87-column residue.

Closing it requires the clean `render` to compute the per-cell budget with HS's
`renderBalanced` proportional-width + per-field-ribbon model — a behavioral logic
change to a clean (headerless) file, i.e. transplanting the exact ported
`render_balanced` expression, which the protocol forbids the dirty room. The
clean crate's `generate.rs` own doc still lists "the accumulated-column wrap
trigger for cells deep on a record line (§3f)" as a GAP, consistent with this
residual. Per "never force a swap": KEPT intact (headers untouched) —
`handlers/dot.rs` (its `render_balanced` IS the faithful proportional-width
engine), `graph/{abbreviation,repr,simplify,options}.rs` (these also stay
independently required: the system->graph mapping, compression content,
per-rule/per-cluster color hash and abbreviation SELECTION over `LNTerm` are
solver-side content, and the clean abbrev engine's ~7% AC/DH residual means it
does not serve the route end-to-end). `routes_graph::dot_output_for_a_simple_
system` UNCHANGED (still pins the ported `digraph G {` dialect; switching it to
the reference dialect would assert output the blocked swap never produces).
`graph_clean` NOT renamed. Deleted: none.

A future close needs `graph_clean::render::cell_budget` to carry HS's
proportional `renderBalanced` field-width + ribbon model (a clean-side change);
then the `RawRule` seam becomes byte-faithful and the swap + deletion of
`handlers/dot.rs` serialization + the `routes_graph` dialect update land as
specified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All clean. Final state verified: no scratch residue, HS server stopped, `macro_expand.rs` has zero code changes (header-comment migration only, pre-existing), `macros.rs` carries `expand_staged` (headerless), and `graph_clean/render.rs` carries the round-6 group-budget engine.

---

**Round-5 closures for units E (macros) + B (graph) — integration complete. Both re-synced; both swaps KEPT-AND-REPORTED (never forced). Header count 133 → 133 (net 0); no author citations disappeared.**

**E (macros):** Re-synced `crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs` from the round-5 workspace (adds the consumer `expand_staged` staged mode: AccLemma/CaseTest formulas untouched, primary-rule-form only; three mechanical transforms, stays headerless). Probed the rewire through `macros::expand_staged`. The two AccLemma/CaseTest non-expansion captured tests PASS through the clean entry, **but the third gate — `bare_nullary_macro_name_expands` — FAILS**: the clean `expand_term` Var arm checks only `sort == Untagged && nullary`, ignoring `idx`/`typ`, so it over-expands the indexed bare name `konst.1` to the macro body where HS/ported leave it a variable (HS's `nullaryApp` only fires on a fully-undecorated name; `konst:pub` is fine — it parses to `Suffix(Pub)`). The clean crate's test corpus never builds an indexed `VarSpec`, so it never observed this. Fixing it needs an `idx==0 && typ.is_none()` guard added to the clean expander — a logic change to a headerless file the protocol forbids me, and no adapter can un-expand after the fact. Reverted the wiring; ported `macro_expand.rs` (`expand_theory_macros`/`expand_items`/`expand_rule`) KEPT intact.

**B (graph):** Re-synced `graph_clean/{generate,render}.rs` from the round-6 workspace (the only two changed files; closes the round-5 wrap TRIGGER via `cell_budget`/`wrap_cell_budget`/`group_cells` — 23 inline tests pass incl. the `Ack` case). **Rebuilt and ran the live byte gate** against the HS reference server (port 3211, `--port=` equals-form; the oracle script's space-form makes cmdargs misread the port as WORKDIR) on a fresh `Wide` probe captured at `interactive-graph-def/cases/raw/1/1`, driving the actual `RawRule` seam (flat cells → `generate` → `to_dot`). Output was **byte-identical to HS through byte 425 (1766/1766 bytes)** — header, record structure, the `Ack` wrap (round-5's blocker, now reproduced), all `<nK>` ids/ports, ellipses, and edges all match — **then diverges inside the `Big` conclusion cell**: clean's flat group-budget `max(87−36,20)=51` breaks one element early, where HS's proportional `renderBalanced` field width (85) / ribbon (`round(85/1.5)=57`) packs `x8.4` on. Feeding the clean `wrap_cell_budget(Big, 55..57)` reproduces HS exactly, proving the clean fill engine is faithful and only the BUDGET formula is wrong. Closing it needs HS's proportional per-field-ribbon model inside clean `render` — a forbidden clean-file transplant of the ported `render_balanced` expression. Kept `handlers/dot.rs` (its `render_balanced` is the faithful engine) + `graph/{abbreviation,repr,simplify,options}.rs`; `routes_graph::dot_output_for_a_simple_system` left on the ported `digraph G {` dialect.

**Provenance tripwire:** all three re-synced files (`macros.rs`, `graph_clean/generate.rs`, `graph_clean/render.rs`) confirmed headerless after `gen_license_headers.py` (0 files updated).

**Validation (all green):** `cargo build --workspace` 0 errors; `cargo test -p tamarin-parser -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-prover -p tamarin-server` = **844 passed / 0 failed** (incl. the 9 macro_expand captured tests via the kept ported path + 23 graph_clean inline tests); wf fixture suite **21/21/21** vs the v1.13.0 oracle; `gen_license_headers.py --check` **0 stale (133 headers)**.
